persona: She is a 7-year-old student from a middle-class family in Karachi with a light skin tone, and she prefers to keep personal details private rather than talk about what is bothering her. When asked about her vision, she tends to shrug it off with "I am fine" and only shares specifics after gentle prompting, often minimizing how much dim light and bright sun affect her. She sometimes puts off homework or clinic tasks until a parent insists, and lately she seems quietly discouraged, especially after headaches from reading or needing to sit closer in class. Her family is careful about medicine use and she can mix up similar medication names unless an adult double-checks.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressive difficulty seeing in dim light with frequent bumping into objects at dusk for about 1 year.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past year, the child has had increasing trouble navigating in low-light settings (hallways at night, outdoors at dusk) and is slower to adjust when lights are turned off. Parents report more frequent tripping and bumping into furniture in the evening and needing to hold an adult's hand in darker places. Over the last several months, she has also begun to sit closer to the TV/board at school and intermittently complains that lights look "too bright" in sunlight. Symptoms are chronic and gradually worsening rather than sudden. Accompanying issues include occasional headaches after sustained reading and mild eye strain. No eye pain, no eye redness or discharge, no acute vision blackout episodes, no double vision, no fever, no recent head trauma, and no neurologic deficits such as weakness, seizures, or altered consciousness.

persona: She is a 24-year-old playlist curator with tech-industry skills who prizes education and keeps a competitive, outgoing face at work despite feeling privately shaken by past unjust treatment. With limited English, she leans hard on translated search results and forums, arriving convinced she already knows the cause of her fatigue and stomach issues, and she pushes confidently for specific labs, imaging, or medications while brushing off details that do not match her theory. She follows the Philadelphia Phillies, tries to stay active with occasional discus training when energy allows, and financial strain from relying on child support and living separated adds to her stress and uneven routines.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressive fatigue with reduced appetite and intermittent abdominal bloating for about 8 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 8 months, she has noticed increasing tiredness, reduced exercise tolerance, and poor appetite with early satiety, initially intermittent but now occurring most days. She reports episodic right upper abdominal discomfort and post-meal bloating with looser stools several times per week, along with gradual unintentional weight loss. In the last 6 to 8 weeks she has also observed occasional easy bruising and mild gum bleeding when brushing, and coworkers have commented that her eyes sometimes look slightly yellow, though this is not constant. She notes occasional itchy skin and scattered small red spots on the upper chest that appear more prominent after hot showers. No black tarry stools, no vomiting blood, no persistent high fever, no severe confusion or daytime somnolence beyond poor sleep, no chest pain or shortness of breath, and no recent travel-related diarrheal illness. No known pregnancy; denies pelvic pain or abnormal vaginal discharge.

current_general_status.diet: Decreased appetite; tends to eat quick, practical meals and often skips breakfast; occasional nausea after alcohol-heavy evenings.
current_general_status.sleep: Chronic insomnia related to stress and irregular work hours; sleeps lightly and wakes frequently.

persona: He is a 30-year-old quality control specialist who values scientific evidence and tends to be quiet but cooperative, though lately he seems distressed and a bit aggrieved about feeling "off" around his eye. He often struggles to describe symptoms clearly, relying on vague words and a jumbled timeline, and sometimes uses simple signing or gestures to fill in what he cannot explain. He lives in a shared household where he helps manage day-to-day responsibilities, eats a fairly high-salt takeout diet during stressful weeks, avoids subscriptions and unnecessary recurring costs, and unwinds by playing with his pets when he can.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Noticed a new small lump on the upper eyelid with mild gritty sensation starting about 6 days ago after a week of increased eye irritation.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Initially a small, firm, painless bump within the upper eyelid that felt mobile under the lid. Over the past several days it has gradually enlarged and causes intermittent foreign-body sensation and mild tearing, with occasional blur like a thin film when blinking, especially late in the day. He reports chronic eye redness and irritation for months with frequent rubbing. No significant eyelid warmth, no marked tenderness to touch, no purulent discharge, no fever, no severe eye pain, no photophobia, and no acute vision loss. No recent eye trauma and no contact lens use.

persona: She is a 72-year-old woman who is divorced and lives on a tight budget, but stays involved by doing part-time community mentoring and occasional evaluating work when she can. She values giving back and keeps a quiet respect for her family roots, and lately she has been focused on regaining a sense of control by practicing new routines hands-on, especially with devices and daily schedules. When she talks with clinicians, she is direct and factual, using simple words and answering questions clearly; she will read handouts if you recommend them and prefers quick, practical next steps.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent episodes of sudden unresponsiveness with shaking and worsening memory and confusion over the past 18 months, with more frequent events in the last 3 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 18 months, the patient and family report gradually worsening short-term memory with repetitive questioning, misplacing items, difficulty recalling recent conversations, and increasing trouble following multi-step directions. Over the same period she has had intermittent stereotyped spells: brief staring with lip smacking and impaired awareness lasting about 1 to 2 minutes, sometimes followed by marked confusion, fatigue, and fluctuating attention for several hours. In the last 3 months, two events involved sudden loss of consciousness with generalized stiffening and rhythmic limb jerking, with tongue soreness afterward and prolonged post-event disorientation. She notes worsening disorientation in unfamiliar places, occasional word-finding difficulty, and decreased ability to manage complex tasks (finances, scheduling). Sleep has been chronically poor with frequent awakenings, and sleep disruption often precedes spells. Accompanying symptoms include intermittent headaches, mood irritability, and daytime sleepiness. She denies persistent focal weakness, persistent numbness, new facial droop, continuous vertigo, fever, neck stiffness, chest pain, palpitations preceding events, exertional syncope, or recent head trauma; there is no consistent association with standing quickly or prolonged heat exposure.

current_general_status.diet: Appetite mildly reduced; eats regular meals with family support; prefers quiet meals and does not watch television.
current_general_status.sleep: Chronic sleep disorder with difficulty initiating and maintaining sleep; frequent nighttime awakenings; daytime sleepiness; currently trying to learn CPAP machine use but adherence is inconsistent.
current_general_status.excretion: No major change reported; occasional constipation.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional loss of about 3 kg over 6 months.

persona: She is a newborn, so her parents speak for her, arriving visibly anxious and extremely talkative, jumping between details, interrupting with worst-case fears, and repeatedly asking for reassurance. They are proactive but constructive skeptics, wanting clear explanations and affordable options as they navigate a recent move to a new city while seeking stable housing and prioritizing community safety. With a protective, health-conscious mindset shaped by past displacement from armed conflict, they lean on a small network that includes local government contacts for resources, yet still worry they are missing something and ask for step-by-step guidance.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Within the first day of life, caregivers noticed bluish discoloration around the lips and increased work of breathing during feeding.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Soon after birth, the infant developed intermittent perioral cyanosis and tachypnea, most noticeable with crying and feeds, with poor endurance at the bottle/breast and frequent pauses. Over several hours the breathing effort became more apparent with mild chest retractions, and oxygen saturation readings at bedside were noted to be low by staff. Accompanying findings include weak feeding, increased sleepiness between feeds, and reduced urine output compared with expected intake. No fever, no vomiting, no diarrhea, no choking episode, no apneic spells reported, and no cough or nasal congestion to suggest an acute upper respiratory infection; no generalized rash or lethargy out of proportion to feeds to suggest sepsis. Maternal rubella history noted; current newborn exam has no obvious syndromic features (no cataracts seen, no petechiae/purpuric rash, no hepatosplenomegaly), and hearing screen status is pending.

current_general_status.diet: Feeding poorly with early fatigue and frequent pauses; takes small volumes and tires easily.
current_general_status.sleep: More sleepy than expected between feeds; arousable with stimulation.
current_general_status.excretion: Urine output decreased: 1 wet diaper over the last 8 hours (within the first day of life); stools passed once since birth.
current_general_status.weight_change: Too early to assess beyond expected immediate post-birth changes; birthweight documented as 3.2 kg, with no documented significant weight loss yet.

medical_history.long_term_medication: None.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: None.

personal_history.obstetric_history: Born at term to an older mother with diabetes during pregnancy. Mother reports rubella infection in early pregnancy (history unconfirmed by records). Delivery occurred during hurricane/typhoon season with disrupted prenatal visits and increased stress; delivery otherwise uncomplicated. Birthweight 3.2 kg. Apgar scores 8 at 1 minute and 9 at 5 minutes. Newborn exam elements relevant to congenital rubella: bedside eye exam without cataracts noted; no petechiae/purpuric rash; no hepatosplenomegaly palpated; newborn hearing screen not yet completed/awaiting prior to discharge/transfer. No known birth trauma.

persona: She is a 16-year-old high school student from an affluent family who likes things steady and familiar, and she tends to be passionate about what she believes is right. She is protective of her digital privacy and prefers using her iPhone, keeping most personal details limited to what feels necessary. She speaks in a direct, factual way, describing what she notices in simple terms and answering questions clearly without drifting off topic. She trains for pole vault and usually pushes through fatigue, but the recent thirst, nonstop peeing, and weight loss have started to affect school focus and workouts.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent excessive thirst and frequent urination with increased appetite and unintentional weight loss over the past 2 months, worsening over the last 2 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last 2 months she has had progressively increased thirst with frequent water intake, frequent daytime urination and waking at night to urinate, and increased hunger despite ongoing weight loss and fatigue. Over the past 2 weeks she reports decreased concentration at school, occasional blurry vision, and reduced exercise tolerance during strength training. She had a viral upper respiratory illness about 6 weeks ago with sore throat, low-grade fever, and myalgias that resolved, after which the thirst and urination became more noticeable. She denies frank vomiting, severe abdominal pain, deep or rapid breathing, fruity breath odor, syncope, chest pain, dysuria, flank pain, persistent cough, or current fever. No known recent head trauma or use of glucocorticoids. No recent polyuria due to diuretics. Given urine ketones (2+) and low bicarbonate on CMP (CO2 19 mEq/L), evaluate for evolving/early DKA with full electrolytes including anion gap calculation, serum beta-hydroxybutyrate, and venous blood gas (pH/bicarbonate). If DKA criteria are met (elevated anion gap metabolic acidosis and significant ketonemia/acidemia), manage in ED/hospital with IV fluids/insulin per protocol; if DKA is ruled out and she remains clinically well-appearing and tolerating oral intake, she is stable for outpatient insulin initiation with strict return precautions.

current_general_status.diet: Increased appetite with frequent snacking; prefers home-prepared, farm-sourced foods but craves sugary drinks recently to relieve thirst.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to waking multiple times to urinate; feels tired during the day.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination markedly increased including nocturia; bowel movements regular without diarrhea.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss over 2 months (clothes fitting looser).

persona: She is a 28-year-old homeowner who runs her own small marketing-focused business and still keeps a longtime side interest in archaeology and legends, hoping to eventually pivot into film work. She values independence and tends to make decisions by simplifying choices into a few practical options, though she’s often disillusioned after years of feeling like her health concerns get brushed off. In visits she’s a poor historian: she describes symptoms as “weird” or “off,” mixes up timelines, and gestures broadly rather than pinpointing details, and she frequently shows up late or forgets appointments despite trying to take a preventive approach.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent episodes of nighttime wheezing and chest tightness with progressive difficulty swallowing and regurgitation for about 8 months, worsening over the past 6 weeks after a period of marked stress.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 8 months, the patient reports paroxysmal wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness that are most prominent at night and in the early morning, often preceded by a sour/bitter taste in the mouth and regurgitation of undigested food or saliva when lying down. She describes an irritant, non-productive nocturnal cough with occasional choking sensations during sleep and intermittent hoarseness on waking. In parallel, she has developed gradually worsening dysphagia to solids and now occasionally to liquids, needing extra time to eat and sometimes using water or positional changes to help food pass; symptoms are worse with rapid eating, late meals, and emotional upset. She endorses retrosternal pressure/burning discomfort after meals and episodic non-cardiac chest pain. She notes reduced intake due to fear of symptoms and an unintentional weight loss over several months. She has chronic heartburn symptoms and reports being told previously that she has ongoing inflammation in the esophagus. She denies fever, hemoptysis, pleuritic chest pain, leg swelling, syncope, persistent vomiting of blood, black stools, or a single sudden severe choking event. She also denies known food bolus impaction requiring emergency removal, recent travel-related infection, or new exposure to pets/mold beyond baseline.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meals with frequent late-night eating and fast/processed foods; currently eating smaller portions and softer foods to avoid symptoms; drinks water to help swallowing.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to nocturnal cough/wheezing and regurgitation; uses extra pillows and avoids lying flat when symptoms are bad.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements regular; no reported blood in stool; urination normal.

persona: He is a 12-year-old middle-income student who was raised to be respectful to adults and expects a professional, confidential conversation when something feels wrong. He talks fast and at length, blurting out fears that it could be cancer or something life-threatening, then veering into details about school, a news story he saw, and how standing around all day or playing pickleball might have “triggered” it. Home feels busy and stressful in a crowded single-parent household, so he seeks constant reassurance and asks for clear, evidence-based explanations while still reacting emotionally.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: New onset left-sided scrotal heaviness and mild aching discomfort noticed after standing for a prolonged period at school starting 2 days ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: He reports a new, intermittent dull ache and sense of heaviness in the left side of the scrotum, most noticeable late in the day after prolonged standing and walking; it is less noticeable after resting or lying down. Over the last 48 hours, he has become more aware of a soft fullness on the left when upright, without sharp pain. No dysuria, urinary frequency, hematuria, penile discharge, fever, nausea/vomiting, abdominal pain, groin bulge, recent cough/straining, rash, or history suggestive of acute torsion (no sudden severe pain). No recent direct scrotal trauma. No back/flank pain to suggest renal colic. Symptoms do not wake him from sleep.

persona: She is a 52-year-old retired former food journalist who is rebuilding her life and prides herself on getting the facts right, even when she feels disappointed and a bit abandoned by how quickly things have changed lately. Her name in this record may be de-identified and should not be used to infer sex. She is thrifty and practical, prefers walking when she can, and uses an Android phone for reminders; she also relies on audiobooks since sitting down to read feels harder with her fatigue. In visits she is direct and factual, describing what she can and cannot do in plain language, but she sometimes has trouble remembering multi-step instructions and appreciates short, written take-home notes.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent cough with gradually worsening shortness of breath for about 4 months, initially noticed after a series of colds and has not returned to baseline.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Main symptoms include a persistent cough that is worse at night and with exertion, intermittent blood-streaked sputum over the past 6 weeks, and progressive dyspnea that has advanced from climbing stairs to becoming short of breath with routine household activity. She reports episodic right-sided chest tightness and dull chest pain with deep breaths, and occasional localized wheeze noted by family. Over the past month she has had several episodes of low-grade fever and increased sputum that at times becomes thick and yellow-green, with transient improvement after outpatient antibiotics but rapid recurrence. Accompanying symptoms include reduced exercise tolerance, fatigue, decreased appetite, and occasional hoarseness. She denies acute pleuritic onset with sudden severe dyspnea, denies calf pain or unilateral leg swelling, denies syncope, denies paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, and denies known tuberculosis exposure; no sustained high fever with rigors, no vomiting, and no recent choking event.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite; eating smaller meals, prefers soft foods due to fatigue with chewing; adequate fluid intake encouraged by family.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented by cough and worry; sleeps about 4 to 5 hours nightly with frequent awakenings.

persona: She is a 46-year-old former prosecutor who is resilient and hardworking but currently unstable in housing and finances, which makes it hard to keep routines and protect her privacy. She talks fast and anxiously, jumping between symptom details, worries about something life-threatening, and side stories about misplaced keys, news she read during lockdown, and the stress of trying to keep things together. She seeks steady reassurance, prefers structured explanations and step-by-step plans, and feels comforted when the clinician checks understanding. Despite low energy and unpredictable meals, she tries to stay active with modified martial arts and is committed to collaborative co-parenting.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent upper abdominal burning/bloating with intermittent easy bruising and gum/nose bleeding over the past 8 months, worsening over the last 3 weeks after a period of increased work stress and irregular meals.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 8 months, she reports episodic epigastric discomfort described as burning and pressure after meals, early satiety, bloating, frequent belching, and sour regurgitation, with flares every few weeks; symptoms have become more frequent over the last 3 weeks with occasional nausea. During the same period she has noticed progressively easier bruising on her arms/legs, scattered pinpoint red spots on the lower legs, and intermittent gingival bleeding when brushing; she has had several brief nosebleeds in the last month. Menstrual flow has become heavier than her baseline with passage of small clots for the last two cycles. She denies fever, persistent vomiting, severe right lower quadrant pain, jaundice, or recent travel-related diarrheal illness. She denies chest pain, shortness of breath at rest, focal neurologic deficits, severe headache, black tarry stools, or vomiting blood. No known recent new medications, over-the-counter herbal supplements, or heavy NSAID use beyond occasional ibuprofen a few times per month.

persona: The patient is a 46-year-old immigrant worker who used to make steady money writing a music column, but lately has been displaced and is trying to pick up part-time work while getting around mostly on foot. The patient speaks in a direct, factual way, using plain words and sticking to what is felt day to day, though there is a haunted, guilt-tinged edge when symptoms interrupt sleep or intimacy. The patient tends to latch onto whatever seems popular or "new" to try for relief, but also values harmony and avoids conflict, retreating into classic Hollywood films and modern UK poetry when feeling neglected or alone.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent urinary frequency, urgency, and burning with urination for about 8 months, worsening again over the past 2 weeks after a period of increased sexual activity.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 8 months he has had waxing-and-waning lower urinary tract symptoms including urinary frequency and urgency, nocturia, dysuria, and a weaker stream with hesitancy and intermittent flow, with a persistent sensation of incomplete emptying. During flares he also reports dull perineal and suprapubic discomfort and occasional pain with ejaculation, which increases his anxiety and worsens sleep. He notes occasional scant whitish urethral moisture after bowel movements. Over the last 2 weeks symptoms have intensified with more nocturia and pelvic discomfort and decreased ability to fully empty. He denies gross hematuria, flank pain, fever or chills, nausea/vomiting, new cough, chest pain, saddle anesthesia, leg weakness, fecal incontinence, penile ulcers, or urethral pus. No acute complete urinary retention to date.

current_general_status.diet: Tries to eat a sustainable diet when possible; recently relies on inexpensive fast food and convenience meals due to unstable housing and limited cooking access; drinks several caffeinated beverages daily.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to nocturia and stress; difficulty falling back asleep after waking.

persona: She is a 38-year-old woman living in the village of Hopkins, separated and working in community program interviewing, where she prefers a practical question-and-answer style to learn new tasks. She keeps her personal life very private and, with money tight, tends to try alternative, do-it-yourself solutions before seeking help, especially for health concerns. In conversation she is stoic and minimizing, insisting she is "fine" and downplaying discomfort until someone close to her pushes her to be seen; she can sound a bit guarded and occasionally envious of friends who seem to have fewer stressors.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent abnormal vaginal discharge with itching and burning for about 4 months, worsening again over the last 10 days after unprotected sexual intercourse.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: She reports intermittent episodes of increased vaginal discharge that is sometimes thin and sometimes frothy, often yellow-green and malodorous, associated with vulvar/vaginal itching and burning. Over the past 10 days she has noticed more irritation, discomfort during intercourse, and mild lower abdominal heaviness with occasional spotting after sex. She also notes urinary frequency and a stinging sensation with urination during flares, without flank pain. Symptoms partially improved in the past with over-the-counter vaginal washes and self-care but recurred. Accompanying symptoms include mild pelvic discomfort and fatigue. She denies fever, chills, nausea or vomiting, severe one-sided pelvic pain, new back pain, visible blood in urine, genital ulcers, vesicular rash, or diffuse joint pain.

current_general_status.diet: Regular meals; recently trying to reduce sugary drinks and refined carbohydrates after being told her blood sugar is high.

persona: He is a 41-year-old man who has been juggling regular dialysis with unstable housing and a tight budget, which often leaves him feeling overlooked even when he is trying hard to do the right thing. He runs a small animal shelter operation and puts a lot of his limited time and money into helping others, especially when it comes to reducing shame around mental health and addiction recovery. In the clinic he speaks in a direct, factual way using simple words, answers questions clearly, and likes explanations through everyday comparisons; he makes quick, practical decisions and tries to protect his sleep routine, though anxiety and his recent symptoms have made rest harder.

Over the next week he noticed dull discomfort/fullness under the right costal margin and urine becoming tea-colored. Three days ago family noted yellowing of the sclera and mild generalized itching; he reports low-grade chills early on that have since resolved. He feels more tired than usual and has had difficulty concentrating, with symptoms worsening in noisy environments. No significant cough, sore throat, or shortness of breath; no chest pain; no severe localized flank pain; no dysuria; no black stools or vomiting blood; no confusion or marked sleep-wake reversal; no known recent acetaminophen overdose.

persona: He is a 38-year-old married martial arts coach with a high income who takes pride in giving to charity and feels a quiet respect for his ancestors. He speaks in a direct, matter-of-fact way, using plain words to describe what hurts and when, and he answers questions clearly without going off on tangents, even if it takes him a bit longer to grasp complicated explanations. Lately he has seemed more empathetic and a little down, leaning on puzzles to unwind between night shifts, while also dealing with sensitive skin and thinking about simple fall-prevention fixes at home as he plans for a future move into farm management.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: New onset of penile curvature with painful erections starting about 3 weeks ago after a minor sexual injury.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: About 3 weeks ago, during intercourse, he felt a sudden sharp pain and noticed mild swelling; bruising was minimal and resolved over several days. Over the following 1 to 2 weeks, he developed pain specifically during erections and began to notice a new upward/leftward bend during erection with a sense of tightness at mid-shaft. He reports a palpable tender, firm area along the dorsal aspect of the penile shaft that feels like a small cord or nodule. Erections are still achievable but less rigid than before, and he sometimes avoids sexual activity due to discomfort and worry. Accompanying symptoms include increased anxiety and reduced sleep quality due to nighttime painful erections. He denies fever, urethral discharge, dysuria, hematuria, testicular pain, groin rash/ulcers, recent new genital lesions, or acute inability to urinate. No perineal trauma, no recent catheterization, and no persistent severe penile swelling at rest.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meals due to night shift work; tends to snack and uses convenience foods, trying to reduce carbohydrates for glucose control.

persona: The patient is a 56-year-old regulatory officer in Footscray who is financially comfortable and prefers decisions guided by clear scientific evidence, but still wants a person to walk through choices rather than relying on apps or portals. Separated and privately sensitive about social acceptance, the patient comes across as steady and resilient, shaped by past hard periods and sustained recovery from alcohol. The patient speaks in a direct, factual way, describing what can and cannot be seen and when headaches or eye strain hit, without using technical terms. The patient tries to protect the heart with simple routines, and also cares about climate-related health preparedness.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Gradually worsening blurred vision for both distance and near tasks over the past 2 years, more noticeable in the last 3 months after prolonged reading and screen use.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Reports progressive difficulty seeing road signs and TV captions, with intermittent squinting and eye strain after long periods of reading or phone/computer work; near work also triggers headaches and a sense of visual fatigue by late afternoon. Describes mild ghosting/haloing around lights at night, especially when driving in rain, and fluctuating clarity that improves briefly with resting the eyes. Symptoms are chronic and recurrent, with temporary worsening during weeks of heavy near work. Accompanied by occasional dryness and watering. Denies sudden curtain-like vision loss, new marked increase in floaters, flashes of light, eye pain, marked redness, purulent discharge, acute neurologic deficits, or recent head/eye trauma.

current_general_status.diet: Frequent fast food; irregular vegetable intake; drinks coffee most days.
current_general_status.sleep: Light, fragmented sleep; averages about 6 hours nightly; fatigue worsens after long screen time.
current_general_status.excretion: No reported bowel or urinary changes.
current_general_status.weight_change: Gradual weight gain over several years; no rapid unintentional weight loss.

Dry eye symptoms with prolonged screen use
Alcohol use disorder in remission

persona: She is a 42-year-old woman who used to work help desk and is now trying to find part-time work that feels manageable, preferring practical, hands-on solutions over long explanations. Quiet and sharp, she downplays what she is going through with a steady "I'm fine" tone, only showing up because a family member insisted, and she tends to minimize symptoms so she does not worry anyone. Since her transplant and HIV history, she keeps a tight routine but admits to frequent fast food, occasional impulse purchases, and a soft spot for charity, while her fascination with stunt driving stays safely on the sidelines.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent episodes of low-grade fever, profound fatigue, and intermittent blurred vision with new floaters over the past 3 months, with worsening over the last 10 days.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 3 months, she reports waxing-and-waning fevers, marked fatigue, diffuse myalgias, and reduced exercise tolerance, initially attributed to stress and returning to work. Over the last 10 days she developed worsening blurred vision in the right eye with floaters and mild photophobia, and has had intermittent non-bloody diarrhea with crampy abdominal pain and decreased appetite. She notes occasional dry cough and mild dyspnea on exertion but no pleuritic chest pain. Accompanying symptoms include sore throat at the start of some flares and tender neck nodes. She denies severe headache, focal weakness, seizures, syncope, neck stiffness, or confusion; no hemoptysis; no melena or hematochezia; no dysuria or flank pain; no new rash. She has not noticed acute unilateral leg swelling. Symptoms tend to recur every few weeks, lasting several days, and have been more persistent recently.

current_general_status.diet: Decreased appetite for 1-2 weeks; tolerates liquids and small meals; avoids heavy foods due to nausea.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep with early awakening; uses CPAP but adherence has been inconsistent during recent illness.
current_general_status.excretion: Intermittent loose stools without blood; urine output normal; no urinary symptoms.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss of about 4 kg over 3 months.

persona: She is a 45-year-old broadcast journalist in Saint Thomas, Canada, juggling multiple jobs and a demanding schedule, which has made her sleep and routines inconsistent. She communicates in a direct, factual way, describing what she feels in plain terms and answering questions clearly without drifting into medical jargon. Her decision-making is modern but also leans holistic, so she is open to practical lifestyle changes alongside medication, though she can come off emotionally distant when stressed or grieving. Outside of work, she decompresses by cooking, volunteers when she can, and carries private insurance while advocating for food allergy awareness in local schools.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Sudden onset of severe pain, redness, and swelling of the right big toe joint starting early this morning (about 10 hours ago) after an intense workout the day prior and beer intake overnight; reports having had a few prior brief self-limited similar episodes in the same toe over the past 1–2 years that resolved without medical evaluation.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Abrupt, burning, stabbing pain centered at the base of the right great toe with rapid swelling, marked warmth, and erythema; pain worsened over a few hours to the point she cannot tolerate bedsheet contact and has difficulty bearing weight. Reports mild subjective chills and fatigue but no documented high fever. No recent skin break, no purulent drainage, and no known puncture injury. No calf pain or unilateral leg swelling, no chest pain or shortness of breath. No dysuria or flank pain. No chronic morning stiffness of multiple small joints, no preceding diarrheal illness, and no new rash. She notes intermittent post-meal bloating and has been trialing a low-FODMAP diet, but this has not changed the acute joint symptoms.

current_general_status.diet: High intake of red meat and organ meats on most days; recent weekend included steak and liver dishes. Low-FODMAP trial for GI bloating; not specifically restricting purine-rich foods.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep last night due to severe toe pain; otherwise variable sleep with high-stress work schedule.
current_general_status.excretion: Normal bowel movements; urinates frequently at night at baseline, which she attributes to diabetes; no gross hematuria.
current_general_status.weight_change: Gradual weight gain over the past year; no acute weight loss.

persona: She is a newborn girl in Porrentruy whose caregivers tend to be stoic and minimizing, repeatedly saying she is “probably fine” and only seeking care after a family member insisted. They speak in short, restrained answers and avoid sounding alarmed, even while noting she has been fussier, sleeping poorly, and feeding less strongly since the umbilical area changed. With limited technology access, they are cautious about sharing photos or personal details and ask pointed questions about privacy and who can view records. They prefer straightforward, practical instructions they can follow at home.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Redness and swelling around the umbilical stump with pus-like drainage starting about 18 hours ago, noticed during routine diaper change shortly after the cord began separating.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Caregivers report new onset redness around the umbilical stump that has spread slightly to the surrounding skin with increasing swelling and warmth. A yellow-green, foul-smelling purulent discharge has been noted, with the diaper sometimes stained. The infant has been more fussy and difficult to console since last night and is feeding less vigorously than usual, with shorter feeds. Caregivers deny vomiting, diarrhea, cough, rhinorrhea, difficulty breathing, skin rash elsewhere, or lethargy/poor arousability. No witnessed seizure activity. No known trauma to the abdomen. History suggests cord care in an unsterile setting during separation, including application of non-sterile substances to the stump.

current_general_status.diet: Breastfeeding with some expressed milk; intake decreased over the past day compared with prior feeds.
current_general_status.sleep: More restless sleep with frequent waking and crying since symptom onset.
current_general_status.excretion: Wet diapers present but slightly fewer than baseline; stools unchanged per caregivers.
current_general_status.weight_change: No documented weight change since hospital discharge; caregivers have not noted obvious weight loss.

persona: An 82-year-old woman with limited resources, she relies on rides from family or friends and tries to stay independent despite aching hands and knees. In conversation she is shy at first but quickly becomes rapid and very verbose, jumping from worries about frightening news stories to detailed accounts of damp rooms, birds, and every small bodily sensation, often interrupting to ask if it could be something deadly. She is determined and wellness-focused, prefers organic remedies when she can, and reads medication labels carefully, valuing peace, justice, and equality while often mentioning her interest in education law.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressively worsening shortness of breath with dry cough over the past 8 months, with recurrent flare-ups after spending time in a damp rented room and around pet birds.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Reports an insidious onset of persistent dry cough and exertional dyspnea that gradually worsened over 8 months; over the last 6 weeks she has had several episodes of increased breathlessness and chest tightness within hours to the next day after cleaning moldy areas, handling stored hay/compost for gardening, or being near bird cages/feathers in shared housing. During flare-ups she notes fatigue, chills, reduced appetite, and occasionally low-grade subjective fever; she also describes unintentional weight loss and reduced exercise tolerance compared with last year. Denies purulent sputum, hemoptysis, pleuritic chest pain, leg swelling, orthopnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, recent immobilization, calf pain, or known aspiration events. No wheezing noted by the patient, and no recent sick contacts suggesting an acute viral illness.

current_general_status.diet: Decreased appetite; eats smaller meals, avoids heavy foods due to breathlessness on exertion.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to nocturnal cough; uses extra pillows but not clearly orthopneic.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel and bladder habits generally regular; no dysuria or diarrhea.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss over several months.

persona: He is a 28-year-old creative lead who tries to project a smiley, upbeat front, but privately feels socially anxious and easily embarrassed in clinical settings. Financially he has been struggling and is careful about recurring costs, avoiding subscriptions and leaning on basic health coverage, while still valuing charity and keeping a strong respect for family traditions and ancestors. He arrives with a confident, self-diagnosing “internet doctor” tone, citing searches and past bouts as proof, downplaying details that do not match his theory and pushing for specific antibiotics or tests; when challenged, he can get defensive but will pause to meditate and regain composure.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Developed worsening sore throat with foreign-body sensation and new migratory joint pain and swelling after repeated cold-and-rain exposure about 10 days ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: About 10 days ago, after commuting in heavy rain and prolonged cold exposure, he noticed throat dryness, scratchy pain with swallowing, and a persistent foreign-body sensation. Over the next several days he developed intermittent low-grade fever and fatigue, with chronic-looking throat irritation and occasional bad breath. Around day 4 to 5, he began to experience migratory pain, warmth, and swelling in large joints starting in one ankle and then involving the opposite knee and a wrist; symptoms shift locations over hours to a day, with morning stiffness and reduced range of motion that improves somewhat with gentle movement but flares after prolonged standing. He reports difficulty walking to the park and climbing stairs due to knee and ankle discomfort. Accompanying symptoms include mild chills, decreased appetite, and headache. He denies chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations, fainting, rash, eye redness or pain, painful urination or urethral discharge, recent diarrhea, significant trauma, or a single joint that is persistently red and exquisitely painful.

current_general_status.diet: Appetite mildly reduced; prefers soft foods and warm drinks due to throat discomfort; irregular meals during workdays.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep with sleep deprivation, worsened by a high-noise living environment and anxiety; wakes frequently.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements regular; urination normal without dysuria.
current_general_status.weight_change: No significant weight change noted over the past few weeks.

previous_diseases: Recurrent episodes of sore throat with positive/treated hemolytic streptococcal infections since adolescence
Occasional seasonal allergic rhinitis
medical_history.long_term_medication: No daily prescription medications; uses over-the-counter ibuprofen intermittently for joint pain and throat discomfort. Recently completed or partially completed short antibiotic courses in the past for throat infections, with inconsistent adherence.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: No prior surgeries; no significant joint trauma reported.
medical_history.vaccinations: Routine childhood vaccinations; influenza vaccine not taken every year.

persona: He is a 29-year-old working student and former serviceman who takes pride in staying active, trying to keep some basketball practice in his routine even when his body feels "off." He values work-life balance but is cautious about overcommitting, and he often needs reminders for appointments because he loses track of dates and details. In conversation he is a poor historian, describing discomfort as "weird" or "uncomfortable" and giving a messy timeline while gesturing broadly to his back, so clinicians usually have to ask focused follow-up questions. At home he is a strict, rules-first parent to his newborn and unwinds with chip tune music and cultural studies when he can.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent low back pain with increasing fatigue for about 4 months, worsened over the past 3 weeks after minor strain while lifting at home.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 4 months, the patient reports intermittent dull lumbar and mid-back pain that initially occurred after prolonged standing and improved with rest; over time it became more frequent and now occurs at night and with minimal activity. During the last 3 weeks, pain intensified after a minor strain while lifting supplies for his newborn, with occasional sharp pain when bending and a subjective sense of decreased height and stooped posture. He also notes persistent fatigue, morning stiffness in several small joints of the hands with episodic swelling, and intermittent facial redness after sun exposure. He denies high fever, productive cough, hemoptysis, chest pressure, syncope, focal neurologic deficits, saddle anesthesia, new bowel or bladder incontinence, dysuria, or recent major trauma. No recent prolonged immobilization, and no known current infection symptoms.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite with irregular meals due to housing instability; mostly plant-based diet with occasional animal products; reports low intake of calcium-rich foods.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep due to back pain and newborn care; wakes multiple times nightly.
current_general_status.excretion: Intermittent foamy urine and nocturia; bowel movements regular without blood.

Renal involvement with prior episodes of proteinuria consistent with inflammatory kidney disease (previously told he has impaired kidney function)
Vitamin D deficiency treated intermittently

persona: She is a 42-year-old salaried safety assessor who often carries the financial load at home, and the strain of debt has left her feeling emotionally overshadowed. In conversation she speaks quickly and at length, interrupting with worst-case fears and extra details from work, her routines, and things she has skimmed in short pamphlets, needing frequent reassurance before she can move on. She tends to make decisions based on trust in clinicians, leaning on discipline and sportsmanship from years of recreational field hockey, and she has a steady interest in engineering history and the industrial revolution.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Sudden worsening mid-to-lower back pain after lifting a heavy box 5 days ago, with increasing fatigue over the past 3 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last 3 weeks she has developed progressive fatigue, reduced exercise tolerance, and intermittent lightheadedness with a noticeable decline in work stamina. Five days ago, after lifting a heavy box, she experienced abrupt onset of sharp mid-to-lower back pain that persists and is worse with movement and prolonged standing; she reports difficulty sleeping due to pain and had been using over-the-counter ibuprofen with partial relief but has now stopped/been advised to avoid NSAIDs due to renal impairment. Over the past week she has also noticed increased thirst and more frequent urination, mild constipation, and occasional nausea; her partner noted she seemed more forgetful and sluggish at times. She reports mild swelling around the eyelids on waking and occasional ankle swelling by evening. She has had two upper-respiratory infections in the past 2 months with slower-than-usual recovery. She denies fever, productive cough, pleuritic chest pain, dysuria, gross hematuria, black or bloody stools, focal weakness, saddle anesthesia, new bowel or bladder incontinence, recent long-distance travel, or known pregnancy.

current_general_status.diet: Strict vegan; appetite decreased over the past 2 weeks, taking mostly soups and smoothies due to fatigue.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep due to back pain; wakes multiple times nightly.

persona: He is a newborn boy, and his parents have a steady home life and focus strongly on peace, fairness, and treating people equally. They make decisions by going with their gut, but when talking with clinicians they stay direct and factual, using simple words and answering questions clearly without rambling. Over the past day they have been worried and a bit shaken by how suddenly his eyes started looking cloudy with lots of tearing and how strongly he reacts to bright light, which has made him harder to soothe and sleep more fitfully.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Parents report that shortly after birth (within the past day), the infant developed cloudy-appearing eyes with excessive tearing and marked light sensitivity.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Since delivery, caregivers noticed a ground-glass/cloudy appearance of the corneas in both eyes, frequent tearing, and eyelid squeezing/spasm that worsens with bright light. Over the last several hours the infant has become increasingly fussy when exposed to light and is harder to console during eye opening or examination attempts. They also note the eyes appear larger than expected for age. No fever, no lethargy, no poor feeding, no vomiting, no seizures, and no purulent eye discharge; no history of eye trauma or chemical exposure. No respiratory distress; parents mention a mild intermittent cough without cyanosis, which should be assessed preoperatively for anesthesia risk.

current_general_status.diet: Breastfeeding and supplemental formula; feeds slightly less during periods of fussiness but overall taking adequate volumes.

current_general_status.excretion: Normal urine output; stools appropriate for age per caregivers.

persona: He is a 42-year-old man who works as a debtor representative, is attentive to design and aesthetics, and tends to jump into problem-solving mode when something feels wrong, even on a tight budget. He is emotionally sensitive and can get thrown off by small stressors at work, like a stuffy or noisy ventilation system, and he worries about whether clinics have easy mobility access and wheelchair-friendly entrances. When describing symptoms, he is a poor historian, saying his neck and arm feel "weird" or "off" and mixing up whether it started "maybe a few days or a couple weeks ago," often gesturing broadly rather than pinpointing exact spots. He keeps up with regular screenings, and in his free time leans toward artistic, community-activist projects, drawing on a background in medicinal chemistry.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent neck pain with radiating right arm numbness and intermittent grip weakness worsening over the past 3 weeks after a period of excessive fatigue and prolonged desk work.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: For about 1 year he has had episodic neck stiffness and aching that flares after long hours on a laptop/phone and during high-stress work periods. Over the last 3 weeks, symptoms have become more frequent and intense, with pain starting in the lower neck and right shoulder region and radiating down the lateral arm into the thumb and index finger, accompanied by tingling and intermittent numbness. He reports a heavy sensation in the right arm and occasional dropping of small objects, with mild subjective reduction in grip strength during flares. Symptoms worsen with neck extension/rotation, prolonged looking down, and after poor sleep; partial relief with rest, heat, and listening to music. Denies recent fall or acute neck injury, fever, rash, unexplained night sweats, chest pain, shortness of breath, facial droop, slurred speech, gait instability, bowel or bladder incontinence, or bilateral leg numbness/weakness. No persistent hand discoloration, no swelling, and no true loss of hand dexterity between episodes. If a cervical collar is used, it is intended only for short, time-limited use during acute flares/high-demand work tasks (not continuous daily immobilization), with a planned wean as symptoms improve to avoid stiffness and to complement PT/traction.

current_general_status.diet: Ingredient-conscious and reads food labels; appetite generally intact, sometimes skips meals during busy workdays.
current_general_status.sleep: Light and fragmented sleep for several months, worse during project deadlines; neck discomfort sometimes wakes him at night.
current_general_status.excretion: Normal urination and bowel movements; no retention or incontinence.

persona: He is a 56-year-old married man who has been self-employed for years, doing consulting work connected to agricultural chemicals, and he has been uninsured since his income became less predictable. He currently has unstable housing and irregular access to food and fluids, which affects his ability to follow strict diet/hydration routines and to attend visits during standard clinic hours. He speaks in a direct, factual way and prefers clear answers in plain language, asking for frequent updates because he values digital privacy and wants to know exactly what is being documented and shared. He can come across indignant when he feels dismissed or rushed, shaped in part by a difficult childhood where he witnessed violence at home, but he stays cooperative and uses deep-breathing to steady himself. His workload often clashes with clinic hours, and long sitting and limiting fluids tend to make his urinary flares worse.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent urinary frequency and burning with urination with intermittent visible blood in the urine, worsening over the past 3 weeks on a background of similar episodes for about 2 years.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last 2 years he has had intermittent episodes of urinary urgency and frequency with nocturia and a sensation of incomplete bladder emptying, often flaring after prolonged sitting or reduced fluid intake. Over the past 3 weeks symptoms have become more persistent with dysuria, weaker urinary stream, hesitancy, post-void dribbling, and intermittent suprapubic/perineal discomfort; he also reports occasional whitish urethral discharge after straining. He has noticed two brief episodes of pink-to-red urine without clots. He endorses mild discomfort with ejaculation during flares and occasional penile tip pain. No fever, chills, flank pain, nausea/vomiting, scrotal swelling, urethral sores, new back trauma, or acute urinary retention.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meals due to unstable housing; relies on inexpensive prepared foods and sometimes limits fluids to avoid frequent urination.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented by nocturia; averages 4-6 hours nightly.
current_general_status.excretion: Urinary frequency and urgency with hesitancy and weak stream; bowel movements generally regular, occasional constipation when stressed.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss of about 3 kg over the last 2 months, attributed to reduced appetite and unstable living situation.

previous_diseases: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with lower urinary tract symptoms
Chronic/recurrent prostatic inflammation (previously treated with antibiotics and anti-inflammatory medications with partial relief)
History of pelvic radiotherapy for prostate cancer (completed approximately 6 years ago)
Radiation-related urinary irritation symptoms after treatment (improved but intermittently recurrent)
Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder diagnosed in adulthood
Situational anxiety during medical visits
medical_history.long_term_medication: Tamsulosin nightly for urinary symptoms; uses NSAIDs as needed for pelvic discomfort; no current daily antibiotics. Occasionally uses melatonin for sleep. Not consistently adherent due to housing instability.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Prostatic urethral stent placement for obstruction/stricture symptoms about 18 months ago; no major trauma reported.
infectious_diseases: Recurrent urinary tract infections/prostatitis episodes in the past
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date with routine adult vaccines per patient report; received seasonal influenza vaccine this year.

personal_history.smoking: Former smoker; quit about 10 years ago after smoking for roughly 15 years, previously about 10 cigarettes/day.
personal_history.alcohol: Rare alcohol use, about 1-2 drinks/month; avoids more due to religious beliefs.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None.

family_history.similar_illness: Father had significant urinary symptoms requiring prostate surgery in his 60s; no other details known.
family_history.hereditary_diseases: None.

persona: She is a 10-year-old student from a low-to-middle income household who usually keeps to herself and prefers connecting through kid-friendly online creative spaces and games. Since her recent hospitalization and surgery, she has been more anxious and doubtful, often saying her breathing and chest feel "weird" or "off" and pointing broadly rather than describing exactly where it hurts, with a jumbled sense of when things started. She is polite and avoids rude language, tries to be inclusive with others, but needs gentle, repeated questions and reassurance to share details, especially when pain makes her hesitant to take deep breaths or cough.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Sudden onset of shortness of breath and right-sided chest tightness starting about 6 hours ago after being discharged home from surgery 2 days ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Began with increased work of breathing and difficulty taking a deep breath, progressing over several hours to dyspnea at rest with intermittent dry cough and a sensation of chest heaviness. Caregiver reports she avoids deep breaths due to significant upper abdominal/chest wall pain after a recent thoracoabdominal procedure, and she has been less active since discharge. Over the last day she developed thick, hard-to-clear sputum and had several episodes of ineffective coughing. EMS noted decreased breath sounds on the right and mild perioral cyanosis that improved with oxygen. She denies choking on food, vomiting, or known foreign-body aspiration. No high fever, no wheezing history, no generalized hives or facial swelling, and no pleuritic pain radiating to the back. No calf pain or leg swelling.

current_general_status.diet: Poor appetite since surgery; taking small amounts of soft foods and fluids.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented due to pain and anxiety; wakes frequently.
current_general_status.excretion: Urine output slightly decreased; constipation since surgery with minimal bowel movement.
current_general_status.weight_change: No clear recent weight change noted.

previous_diseases: Recent severe blunt trauma from a motor vehicle collision with multiple rib fractures and pulmonary contusion
Respiratory distress requiring ventilatory support during initial hospitalization
Right-sided pneumothorax treated with chest tube during the same admission
Recurrent thick secretions with suspected mucus plugging during ICU stay
Endobronchial obstructing lesion noted on bronchoscopy during hospitalization (awaiting definitive pathology)
medical_history.long_term_medication: None chronically. Current post-discharge meds include scheduled acetaminophen and ibuprofen for pain, a short course of oral opioid (non-codeine/non-tramadol; e.g., oxycodone) as needed for breakthrough pain with caregiver monitoring for sedation/respiratory depression, and a stool softener; intermittent nebulized saline was used in hospital for thick secretions.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Recent thoracoabdominal surgery related to trauma management; chest tube placement for pneumothorax; no prior surgeries before this event.
infectious_diseases: None significant; no known tuberculosis exposure
medical_history.blood_transfusion: Received packed red blood cell transfusion during trauma resuscitation per caregiver report.
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date for age per caregiver; received seasonal influenza vaccine this year.

personal_history.smoking: Never; no vaping. Possible intermittent secondhand smoke exposure from a household visitor.
personal_history.alcohol: None.
personal_history.obstetric_history: Full-term birth without complications; normal developmental milestones.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None known; no cystic fibrosis or primary immunodeficiency reported in first- or second-degree relatives.

persona: He is a 20-year-old male student from a middle-income background who tries to value healthy living on paper, but often slips into neglectful routines like skipping dental care and missing medications when life feels scrutinized or stressful. He speaks rapidly and at length, interrupting with worst-case fears about not being able to breathe, then veering into unrelated details about late-night rideshares, commuting hassles, and how he has not really read up on health information. He is single and takes on a lot at home as a foster parent to one child, which feeds his anxiety and makes him plead for frequent reassurance.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Developed fever with progressive shortness of breath and dry cough starting 6 days ago after several nights of poor sleep and missed doses of antiretroviral therapy and autoimmune immunosuppressive medications for approximately 1 week; not consistently taking opportunistic infection prophylaxis.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Initially noted low-grade fever, fatigue, and a dry, nonproductive cough that seemed mild, then over the past 3 days developed worsening exertional dyspnea and chest tightness, now short of breath with climbing a single flight of stairs. Reports chills, reduced exercise tolerance, and decreased appetite with a few pounds of unintentional weight loss over 2 weeks. Occasional pleuritic chest discomfort but no significant sputum production and no hemoptysis. Denies wheezing history, no sore throat, no rhinorrhea, and no known sick contacts. No leg swelling, no unilateral calf pain, and no sudden sharp tearing chest/back pain. No vomiting or aspiration event, and no recent travel exposures. Notes symptoms feel similar to a prior episode of severe pneumonia that required hospitalization. Physical findings described by patient as 'breathing faster than usual' but no confusion or fainting episodes.

current_general_status.diet: Pescatarian; decreased intake over the last week due to poor appetite.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor and fragmented for the past week due to cough and anxiety about breathing.
current_general_status.excretion: Normal urination; stools slightly less frequent over the last week, no diarrhea.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss over the past 2 weeks (clothes fitting looser).

previous_diseases: HIV infection with advanced immunosuppression (AIDS) with prior opportunistic infections
Previous episode of Pneumocystis pneumonia requiring hospitalization (about 1 year ago)
Autoimmune inflammatory condition treated with immunosuppressive therapy (history of systemic autoimmune disease)
History of major surgery with postoperative complications and prolonged recovery (late teens)
medical_history.long_term_medication: Antiretroviral therapy with inconsistent adherence; intermittent prophylaxis for opportunistic infections in the past but not taking regularly; currently on chronic immunosuppressive therapy for autoimmune disease (e.g., systemic corticosteroid with a steroid-sparing agent).
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Major abdominal surgery in adolescence complicated by postoperative infection and slow wound healing; no other major trauma reported.
infectious_diseases: HIV infection with high viral load on recent testing
Recurrent oral candidiasis in the past
medical_history.blood_transfusion: Received blood transfusion during prior complicated surgery; no known transfusion reactions.
medical_history.vaccinations: Childhood immunizations reportedly complete; has missed recent adult boosters; influenza vaccine not received this season.
