demographics.name: Beverly Cole
demographics.occupation: Research technician at a fertility lab

persona: She is a 34-year-old single mom who keeps her kids at the center of every decision, even while juggling low-income freelance art gigs and a day job as a research technician in a fertility lab after studying at Rutgers. She speaks in a direct, matter-of-fact way, describing what she feels in simple terms and asking practical questions, but she can be critical when plans feel unclear and her mood can swing when stress is high. She tracks her sugar intake, reads scientific articles when she can, and used to do endurance workouts, though lately her energy and breathing feel limited.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent abdominal bloating and lower abdominal discomfort with nausea starting about 10 days after oocyte retrieval from a recent ovarian stimulation cycle (fresh embryo transfer performed; early positive beta-hCG), worsening over the past 4 days.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Patient is undergoing assisted reproduction with exogenous gonadotropin injections and had oocyte retrieval; a fresh embryo transfer was performed, with early positive beta-hCG shortly before presentation (early pregnancy). She reports a very high follicle/egg yield during monitoring and retrieval. About 10 days into the post-trigger/post-retrieval period, she developed progressive abdominal distension, pelvic pressure, early satiety, and intermittent nausea with two episodes of vomiting and loose stools. Over the last 4 days she has noted increasing abdominal girth, reduced appetite, and mild shortness of breath when climbing stairs. She reports decreased urine output compared with baseline and feels more fatigued. hCG luteal support was discontinued/avoided once OHSS was suspected; progesterone-based luteal support is being used instead. She has experienced similar episodes after prior stimulation cycles, including one previously treated with outpatient monitoring and fluid advice. She denies fever, chills, dysuria, flank pain, heavy vaginal bleeding, foul-smelling discharge, severe unilateral pelvic pain, syncope, calf pain/redness, hemoptysis, or chest pain.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced oral intake due to early satiety and nausea; trying small frequent meals and oral fluids.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to abdominal discomfort and anxiety.
current_general_status.excretion: Loose stools intermittently; reports decreased urine volume over the last several days without dysuria.
current_general_status.weight_change: Reports rapid weight gain over the past week with increasing abdominal girth.

previous_diseases: Polycystic ovary syndrome with irregular menses since late teens
Prior episodes of complications after ovarian stimulation requiring close monitoring
Anxiety symptoms, especially around medical visits
medical_history.long_term_medication: Currently on assisted reproduction medications including exogenous gonadotropins; hCG luteal phase support was initially planned/started but was discontinued/avoided once OHSS was suspected (no further hCG doses). Uses progesterone-based luteal support per fertility clinic. Takes a prenatal vitamin. No other chronic daily medications.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Transvaginal oocyte retrieval in prior cycles and again during the recent cycle; no other surgeries or major trauma.
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date on routine adult vaccinations per patient; influenza vaccine this season.

personal_history.smoking: Never smoker.
personal_history.alcohol: Rare alcohol use; none since starting fertility treatment.
personal_history.obstetric_history: G3P2A0 with current early pregnancy after a fresh embryo transfer in the same stimulation cycle; retrieval occurred approximately 10 days prior to symptom onset, and beta-hCG became positive shortly before presentation (early gestation). Prior two term vaginal deliveries without major complications. No history of ectopic pregnancy. Currently followed closely by fertility clinic due to high ovarian response to stimulation and prior post-stimulation complications.
personal_history.menstrual_history: Historically irregular cycles and oligomenorrhea consistent with PCOS; menses often months apart without hormonal regulation. Last normal spontaneous menses prior to the current treatment cycle was several months ago; currently amenorrheic due to pregnancy and treatment hormones.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: Mother with type 2 diabetes; no known hereditary clotting disorders or genetic syndromes reported.

persona: He is a 17-year-old student from Tlacahuixtla, Mexico, in a working-class household, and he tries to keep school and rest in balance even when life feels heavy. He tends to be nostalgic and a bit sensitive, and he can be critical about how public money is spent, but he stays cooperative when he feels listened to. When he talks with clinicians, he is direct and factual, using simple words and giving a clear timeline, and he prefers quick, practical next steps. He relies on an Android phone for reminders and worries about whether his apps and support will keep working.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Developed sudden painful gum swelling with spontaneous bleeding and bad breath about 2 weeks ago, prompting urgent dental/medical evaluation.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 2 weeks he noticed rapidly worsening gum enlargement that now partially covers portions of the tooth crowns, with dark red to pale swollen gums that bleed spontaneously and with gentle brushing or eating. He reports new mouth sores with areas that look ulcerated and occasionally foul-tasting, plus worsening halitosis. Symptoms progressed from mild gum irritation to persistent bleeding and difficulty chewing. He also developed systemic symptoms including intermittent fever, marked fatigue, reduced exercise tolerance, lightheadedness, easy bruising, and occasional nosebleeds. Over the last week he noticed tender neck “glands.” He denies chest pain, shortness of breath at rest, severe localized tooth pain with facial swelling, drooling, difficulty swallowing, coughing blood, black stools, severe headache, focal neurologic deficits, or recent travel-associated exposures. No known recent dental procedures.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite due to oral pain; prefers softer foods; tries to keep meals low in sodium at home but intake has been inconsistent recently.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep from mouth discomfort and fevers; wakes multiple times nightly.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel and urinary habits generally unchanged; no reported blood in urine or stool.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional mild weight loss over the past 2 weeks.

previous_diseases: Recent evaluation for acute hematologic malignancy with associated severe thrombocytopenia and neutropenia (under active workup and early treatment planning)
medical_history.long_term_medication: None routinely. Recently started on mouth rinses and short courses of symptomatic medications as directed; he admits he often forgets to take medications unless reminded by phone alarms or device cues.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: No surgeries. Remote history of psychological trauma; no major physical trauma reported.
medical_history.blood_transfusion: None to date; has been counseled that transfusion support may be needed depending on laboratory trends.
medical_history.vaccinations: Childhood immunizations reportedly up to date; no recent live vaccines.

personal_history.smoking: Denies cigarette or vaping use; notes recent wildfire smoke exposure in his area over the past month.
personal_history.alcohol: Denies alcohol use.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None

persona: He is a 38-year-old man who makes his living consulting in furniture design, with a connoisseur's eye for materials and sustainable sourcing. Separated and fiercely independent, he came in only after a family member insisted, insisting he is "fine" while keeping his ankle rigid and avoiding details that might sound like complaining. He tends to act quickly on instinct, then downplay the consequences, and he sometimes struggles with medical terminology, asking for plain-language explanations. He values inclusivity and routinely pushes for clear, upfront discussions about healthcare costs.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Severe right ankle and hindfoot pain with swelling after a fall from a ladder about 3 hours ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: The patient reports falling from a significant height and landing on his right foot with the ankle forced upward, followed by immediate severe pain below the ankle joint and inability to bear weight. Swelling rapidly increased around the ankle and hindfoot, and he noticed bluish-purple bruising along the front and outer aspect of the ankle. He keeps the ankle still due to pain and notes markedly limited motion. He denies open wounds or skin break, numbness or tingling in the foot, coldness or color change of the toes, pain in the knee or hip, head strike, loss of consciousness, chest pain, shortness of breath, fever, or calf pain. Tetanus status: patient unsure of exact last booster date; immunization record to be verified; tetanus prophylaxis not indicated given no open wound.

current_general_status.diet: Poor appetite since the injury; usually eats light meals and takes a daily multivitamin.
current_general_status.sleep: Disturbed by pain since the injury; typically sleeps 6 to 7 hours.
current_general_status.excretion: No change; normal urination and bowel movements.
current_general_status.weight_change: Chronically underweight; no recent unintentional weight loss reported.

previous_diseases: Obstructive sleep apnea using CPAP at home
medical_history.long_term_medication: No prescription long-term medications; takes a daily multivitamin.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: None.
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date for routine adult vaccines per patient; last tetanus booster date uncertain (reported within past 10 years). Plan: verify tetanus immunization record; tetanus prophylaxis not indicated at this time given no open wound/skin break.

personal_history.obstetric_history: Not applicable (male).

personal_history.smoking: Never smoker.
personal_history.alcohol: Rare, about 1 to 2 drinks per month.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: Mother with type 2 diabetes; otherwise none known.

persona: He is a 47-year-old married man who makes a living hosting events and traveling often, so he tries to stay consistent with exercise even when his meals end up being whatever fits a low-carb plan on the road. He lives on a tight budget, leans on faith to stay grounded, and has gotten a bit numb to day-to-day health issues after years of managing blood pressure, diabetes, and skin flares. When he talks with clinicians, he is direct and factual, using plain language and sticking to what he notices, and he prefers video visits when possible. He learns best by doing, and he wants a clear, step-by-step explanation of what is happening and what to do next.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Gradual worsening of peripheral vision and intermittent blurred vision with eye pressure sensation over the past 8 months, noticed after increasing use of steroid medication.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last 8 months, the patient reports slowly progressive difficulty with side vision, more noticeable at night and while driving, with occasional episodes of blurred vision and a dull ache/pressure sensation in both eyes. He describes intermittent headaches and seeing halos around lights during evening hours, occurring a few times per month and lasting hours, sometimes improving with rest. He has begun bumping into objects on his left side and needs brighter light to read. Symptoms have become more frequent over the past 2 months. He denies sudden severe unilateral eye pain, marked redness, sudden complete vision loss, fever, eye discharge, jaw claudication, focal neurologic deficits, or recent eye trauma. He reports prior clinic measurements showing elevated eye pressure and was told there is reduced drainage of eye fluid; given the associated halos/headaches and examination findings of shallow/narrow angles, these intermittent episodes may reflect intermittent angle-closure physiology coexisting with open-angle glaucomatous damage.

current_general_status.diet: Keto-inclined; tends to choose low-carbohydrate meals when possible but diet consistency varies due to frequent travel/hosting responsibilities.
current_general_status.sleep: Light, fragmented sleep; wakes 1 to 2 times nightly, often due to worry about health and work.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination more frequent at night; bowel movements regular.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight gain of about 4 kg over the last year.

previous_diseases: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (about 6 years)
Hypertension (about 8 years)
Chronic eczema/allergic dermatitis with intermittent flares
Hyperlipidemia
medical_history.long_term_medication: Metformin; an ACE inhibitor for blood pressure; statin. Long-term and recurrent use of corticosteroids (primarily topical for dermatitis, with intermittent oral bursts over recent years).
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Recent minor outpatient surgical procedure with ongoing wound care (site not specified by patient); no history of eye surgery; no significant ocular trauma.
medical_history.vaccinations: Childhood vaccinations completed per patient; influenza vaccine inconsistently; no recent updates recalled.

personal_history.smoking: Never smoker.
personal_history.alcohol: Occasional alcohol, about 1 to 2 drinks on weekends; denies heavy use.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None.

family_history.similar_illness: Father diagnosed with glaucoma in his late 50s; paternal aunt reportedly had progressive vision loss requiring long-term eye drops.
family_history.hereditary_diseases: Type 2 diabetes and hypertension in multiple first-degree relatives.

persona: He is a 76-year-old retired education researcher who still follows local issues and volunteers when he can, preferring practical community help over new rules or red tape. He speaks in a direct, factual way, using plain words to describe what he feels and answering questions clearly, though his mood can seem a bit weighed down and he gets quietly competitive about staying independent. Since losing his spouse recently, he has been grieving but keeps going with a pragmatic routine, leaning on infection-prevention habits and a caregiver when needed. Playing with his pets is his main stress relief.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent episodes of worsening shortness of breath with wheeze and new-onset intermittent palpitations over the past 3 months, with a marked flare after a respiratory infection about 10 days ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: He has a long history of episodic wheezing and chest tightness that typically worsens with colds and smoke exposure. Over the last 3 months, he reports more frequent attacks with shortness of breath at rest, needing to sit upright to breathe during episodes, and reduced exercise tolerance. Since developing a recent cough, sore throat, and increased sputum 10 days ago, he has had several severe bouts of breathlessness with audible wheeze, associated with a racing or irregular heartbeat, lightheadedness, and occasional brief visual dimming; one episode nearly caused a fall. He uses a rescue inhaler more often than usual, which sometimes improves breathing but is followed by shakiness and stronger awareness of heart pounding. During the worst episodes he becomes sweaty and anxious and his caregiver has noted he appears pale. He denies crushing or persistent chest pain, hemoptysis, unilateral leg swelling, sustained fever or rigors, new focal neurologic deficits, or prolonged loss of consciousness.

current_general_status.diet: Appetite decreased during the recent infection; tries to eat regular meals but sometimes skips when breathless; drinks coffee daily.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep with nighttime breathlessness and coughing; sleeps propped up on pillows.
current_general_status.excretion: No diarrhea; urination increased due to diuretic use; no dysuria reported.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional mild weight loss over the last few months, attributed to poor appetite during recurrent flares.

previous_diseases: Chronic bronchial asthma with recurrent exacerbations
Hypertension
Chronic lower-extremity edema treated with diuretics
Recurrent respiratory infections (bronchitis/pneumonia episodes in prior winters)
medical_history.long_term_medication: Inhaled controller therapy used inconsistently; short-acting bronchodilator used frequently during flares; intermittent oral steroids during past exacerbations. Long-term diuretic for edema/blood pressure with prior low potassium episodes; takes potassium supplements irregularly. Avoids injections when possible due to needle phobia. Uses home oxygen during severe breathlessness.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: History of severe burn injury in adulthood requiring skin grafting; no recent trauma.
medical_history.blood_transfusion: Received transfusion during burn management in the past (details unclear).
medical_history.vaccinations: Influenza vaccine some years but not consistently; unsure about pneumococcal vaccination status.

personal_history.smoking: Long-term smoker since youth; currently smokes daily despite counseling, with increased symptoms after smoking.
personal_history.alcohol: Occasional alcohol use; no heavy use reported.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None

family_history.similar_illness: Father had chronic breathing problems in later life; older brother has episodes of irregular heartbeat.
family_history.hereditary_diseases: None

persona: She is a term newborn girl, small for her age, who struggles to finish feeds and often breathes fast, sweats, and sometimes looks a bit blue around her lips during feeding, with mild yellowing and a faint bumpy rash noticed soon after birth. Her parents recently settled in Catalonia and are very family-first and community-minded; they sound a little scarred by past experiences and try to be gentle and trauma-aware while advocating for her. They are talkative but direct and factual, describing what they see in plain words, and they prefer clear next steps over numbers and percentages.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Since birth (last several days), caregiver reports poor feeding with rapid breathing and intermittent bluish discoloration around the lips.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Born at term to a mother who was not vaccinated against rubella and had immunosuppression during pregnancy; prenatal course notable for a maternal febrile rash illness in early gestation per caregiver history, with concern for transplacental viral exposure. Since delivery, the infant has had recurrent episodes of tachypnea and sweating with feeds, tires easily, and takes smaller volumes with frequent pauses; symptoms have been persistent with intermittent worsening during feeding. During feeds, caregivers report intermittent perioral cyanosis with brief desaturation episodes and increased work of breathing; there are concerns for pulmonary overcirculation/early congestive heart failure given feeding intolerance, tachypnea, and diaphoresis. Caregiver also notes generalized jitteriness/irritability, reduced responsiveness at times, and a diffuse faint rash with small raised spots noted shortly after birth. Mild yellowing of the skin and sclera has been observed. No reported fever, no cough, no choking episodes, no vomiting of bilious material, no diarrhea, and no known sick contacts since discharge. No seizures witnessed and no bulging of the anterior fontanelle observed by caregiver.

current_general_status.diet: Feeding by breast/formula with reduced intake; tires during feeds and requires frequent breaks.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep with periods of irritability; consolable but settles slowly.
current_general_status.excretion: Wet diapers present but somewhat decreased; stools present without blood.
current_general_status.weight_change: Low birth weight with minimal weight gain since birth per caregiver.

previous_diseases: Neonatal jaundice (ongoing evaluation)
Patent ductus arteriosus and pulmonary artery stenosis (identified on evaluation)
Congenital cataracts confirmed on eye examination; hearing assessment pending
Congenital rubella infection confirmed (IgM positive and virus isolated)
medical_history.long_term_medication: None.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: None.
infectious_diseases: In utero rubella virus exposure/infection via transplacental transmission (maternal illness reported in early pregnancy)
medical_history.vaccinations: Newborn immunizations not yet started or incomplete due to age; mother lacked rubella vaccination per history.

personal_history.smoking: None (neonate). Household smoking exposure not reported.
personal_history.alcohol: None (neonate).
personal_history.obstetric_history: Birth history: term female neonate, small for gestational age; family recently settled in Catalonia, Spain. Pregnancy complicated by maternal immunosuppression and lack of rubella vaccination; caregiver reports a febrile rash illness in early gestation. Delivery without reported birth trauma. No neonatal intensive care stay reported, but close follow-up advised.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None known.

persona: He is a 34-year-old man with a finance background who is currently out of work and stretching a tight budget, yet he stays community-minded by volunteering when his energy allows and quietly giving what he can. Recently widowed, he feels increasingly alienated and tends to keep his grief private, leaning on the responsibility of fostering one child to keep moving forward. In conversation he is stoic and minimizing, insisting he is "fine" and downplaying discomfort, especially after a past painful medical procedure left him wary of clinics. He unwinds with cult-classic films and thoughtful discussions about representation.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent urinary frequency and burning with urination with intermittent blood in urine for about 4 months, and chronic cough with low-grade fevers and weight loss worsening over about 8 months after stopping prior treatment early.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 8 months, the patient reports a persistent cough with daily sputum production that gradually increased, with intermittent streaks of blood in sputum, afternoon low-grade fevers, drenching night sweats, fatigue, and progressive unintentional weight loss. About 4 months ago he developed urinary frequency that became refractory and now occurs day and night, accompanied by urgency, dysuria, suprapubic discomfort, and intermittent terminal hematuria; he also notes cloudy urine and occasional flank aching without severe colicky pain. Symptoms have been recurrent with partial short-lived relief from over-the-counter analgesics and sporadic antibiotics obtained without follow-up, then return and slowly worsen. He denies acute sudden shortness of breath, pleuritic chest pain, syncope, or leg swelling. He denies urethral discharge, new genital ulcers, testicular pain, passage of stones, or a single episode of high fever with rigors. No recent travel; reports long-term close contact with individuals known to have chronic cough in congregate settings.

current_general_status.diet: Poor appetite; often skips meals due to limited finances and low energy; diet low in protein and calories.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to night sweats and needing to urinate frequently.
current_general_status.excretion: Urinary frequency/urgency and dysuria with intermittent hematuria; occasional constipation when intake is low.
current_general_status.weight_change: Gradual unintentional weight loss over months; tracks weights intermittently on a smart scale when available.

previous_diseases: HIV infection with inconsistent follow-up
Prior episode of chronic lung infection treated with a prolonged multi-drug regimen but stopped early due to side effects and poor access
medical_history.long_term_medication: Intermittent antiretroviral therapy in the past with periods of non-adherence; no consistent prophylactic medications. Uses occasional NSAIDs and has taken short courses of antibiotics from walk-in clinics without completing follow-up.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: None.
infectious_diseases: Recurrent oral thrush in the past year
medical_history.vaccinations: Uncertain and incomplete by history; reports hesitancy toward vaccines and has declined several adult immunizations.

personal_history.smoking: Former smoker; quit about 2 years ago after smoking around half a pack per day for about 10 years.
personal_history.alcohol: Occasional alcohol use; denies heavy daily drinking.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None.

persona: He is a 4-year-old boy in Winsted, Connecticut, who loves drawing and building pretend “airplanes” out of blocks, but lately gets tired and cranky more easily. He communicates in simple, direct sentences, and his parents answer questions clearly too, pointing out when the headaches hit, what makes them worse (noise, bright lights, busy daycare), and what helps (a dark, quiet room and rest). His family is close-knit and tries to stay patient and forgiving during hard days, though they admit they feel anxious and cautious since the fall and are watching him carefully.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent headaches with dizziness and sound/light sensitivity starting after a minor head injury about 4 months ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: About 4 months ago, the child fell and struck his head while playing at home; there was brief crying but no prolonged loss of consciousness reported. He was evaluated at the time with normal neurologic exam and head imaging reported as without acute bleeding or fracture. Since then he has had recurrent headaches occurring several times per week, often worse with activity and noisy environments, with associated dizziness, fatigue, and irritability. Parents note increased inattention compared with baseline (difficulty focusing on games/books, more forgetful of simple instructions) and intermittent unsteadiness when tired, without persistent weakness. He is more sensitive to bright light and loud sounds and sometimes covers his ears or asks for lights to be turned off. Symptoms fluctuate and tend to worsen with poor sleep, family stress, and overstimulating settings (busy daycare), and improve with rest and a quiet room. Although there is no current fever, no cough or congestion, no persistent vomiting, no witnessed seizures, no focal limb weakness or facial droop, and no neck stiffness, the prolonged course with intermittent gait unsteadiness and caregiver-reported brief episodes of appearing "dazed"/less responsive prompted a broader evaluation to exclude atypical post-traumatic sequelae and other causes (e.g., occult seizures, inflammatory/infectious processes, or intracranial pressure abnormalities). For this reason, EEG and advanced imaging (including SPECT for functional perfusion patterns) were obtained, and LP/CSF studies were pursued to assess for inflammation/infection and to document opening pressure in the setting of persistent headaches and dizziness. No report of progressive vision loss; no ear discharge or acute severe ear pain.

current_general_status.diet: Picky appetite on headache days; otherwise eats a typical preschool diet and drinks fluids adequately.
current_general_status.sleep: Restless sleep with more nighttime awakenings since the injury; occasionally naps more due to fatigue.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination and bowel movements regular; no new incontinence.
current_general_status.weight_change: No clear weight loss or gain reported.

previous_diseases: Minor traumatic head injury from a fall about 4 months ago (initial evaluation without acute findings)
Severe burn injury in early childhood requiring skin grafting
medical_history.long_term_medication: None.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Skin graft surgery for prior burn injury; minor head trauma from fall as noted.
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date for age per caregiver; family is COVID-cautious.

personal_history.smoking: None (child; no tobacco use).
personal_history.alcohol: Does not drink alcohol.
personal_history.obstetric_history: Full-term birth without major perinatal complications per caregiver; normal early development reported prior to the recent head injury.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None known.

persona: She is a 45-year-old patient coordinator with an affluent, structured life who values honesty and likes when people “play fair,” even in stressful situations. She tends to describe symptoms in broad, vague terms and mixes up timelines, often saying things feel “off” and gesturing to a general area, and she can struggle to remember multi-step instructions. Widowed and raising one child, she is quietly worried but tries to stay hopeful, finding comfort in learning to bake, though she frequently misplaces her phone, keys, or paperwork and gets flustered when routines are disrupted.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Sudden onset of burning epigastric pain that began 5 days ago, most noticeable at night and when the stomach is empty.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Pain is described as a gnawing/burning discomfort in the upper abdomen with a fairly localized tender spot, initially intermittent but becoming daily over the last 3 days. Symptoms are worse late at night and before meals and improve within minutes after eating or after taking over-the-counter acid-suppressing medication. Accompanied by sour taste/acid regurgitation, frequent belching, and episodic nausea without vomiting. Appetite is mostly preserved but she snacks more to avoid pain. She reports a single episode of dark stool (dark-brown to possibly tarry) earlier in the course, without recurrent melena or large-volume bleeding; no known iron use, and she is uncertain if any bismuth-containing products were taken around that time. Documented that the positive FOBT may reflect recent intermittent/occult bleeding despite a clean-based ulcer with no active bleeding seen on EGD. No fever, no persistent diarrhea, no jaundice, no right lower quadrant pain, no chest pressure radiating to arm/jaw, and no painful swallowing.

current_general_status.diet: Prefers a predictable routine; recently eating smaller, more frequent meals and using bland foods to reduce discomfort; increased coffee intake on some nights to stay awake at work.
current_general_status.sleep: Chronic insomnia related to stress; sleep has been worse this week due to nocturnal abdominal pain.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel habits mildly variable with occasional constipation; no persistent watery diarrhea; one episode of darker stool reported, otherwise brown stools.
current_general_status.weight_change: No clear weight loss; slight recent weight gain due to more frequent snacking to relieve discomfort.

previous_diseases: Helicobacter pylori infection diagnosed by stool antigen about 1 month ago; treatment incomplete due to medication access and side effects
Chronic low back pain
Postmenopausal osteoporosis/low bone density
medical_history.long_term_medication: Frequent NSAID/analgesic use (ibuprofen/naproxen most days for back pain for several months). On a bisphosphonate for bone density (e.g., weekly alendronate) for about 1 year; sometimes takes it without enough water or lies down soon afterward due to early-morning schedule (increasing upper-GI irritation risk). Intermittent OTC proton-pump inhibitor or antacid in the past week for stomach pain.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: None.
medical_history.vaccinations: Routine childhood immunizations reported; adult vaccination status uncertain due to limited access to primary care.

personal_history.smoking: Current smoker, approximately 10 cigarettes per day for about 15 years; has attempted to cut down during periods of stress.
personal_history.alcohol: Regular alcohol use, typically 2 to 4 drinks on weekends and occasional weekday drinking for stress relief; denies heavy daily use.
personal_history.obstetric_history: G2P2, both uncomplicated vaginal deliveries; no history of preeclampsia or gestational diabetes.
personal_history.menstrual_history: Perimenopausal with irregular cycles over the past year; last menstrual period about 3 months ago; no intermenstrual bleeding.

family_history.similar_illness: Father had recurrent peptic ulcer symptoms and was treated for an ulcer in his 50s.
family_history.hereditary_diseases: Mother with type 2 diabetes; no known familial cancers or bleeding disorders.

persona: She is a 34-year-old arachnologist with a dark skin tone who keeps her personal life quiet, preferring calm routines like yoga and pilates to stay grounded when political events feel overwhelming. Despite vast financial resources, she is reflective and values forgiveness and justice, trying to make thoughtful choices rather than reactive ones. She often bikes short distances and tracks her vitals on a wearable, but she downplays symptoms with a stoic "I'm fine" and tends to delay care until her spouse insists she get checked.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressively worsening shortness of breath and intermittent chest tightness for about 6 months, with recurrent flare-ups over the last 6 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Symptoms began gradually with exertional breathlessness while walking uphill and a dry cough. Over months, dyspnea progressed to limiting routine activities and occasionally occurring at rest, with episodic worsening associated with a sensation of right-sided chest heaviness and reduced exercise tolerance. She notes intermittent pleuritic discomfort but reports relatively high pain tolerance and tends to delay seeking care. During flares she also feels early satiety and mild fatigue. She has noticed reduced breath sounds when lying on one side and reports that after heavier meals she can feel more bloated/early satiety with a sense of abdominal fullness that makes breathing feel more restricted, which she attributes to limited diaphragmatic excursion in the setting of the large effusion rather than food intake directly triggering dyspnea. Over the same period she reports long-standing skin-colored to pink facial papules over the cheeks/nasal area since her twenties, and a history of recurrent focal seizures since adolescence that are generally controlled but have increased in frequency when sleep-deprived. She also reports intermittent flank fullness and occasional visible blood in urine over the past year. Negative symptoms: denies fever, chills, purulent sputum, wheezing predominance, hemoptysis, acute crushing chest pain radiating to arm/jaw, calf swelling, recent prolonged immobility, or sudden onset pleuritic pain suggestive of an acute embolic event.

current_general_status.diet: Often intends to eat heart-healthy foods but frequently relies on fast food while studying abroad; overall high-fat intake, sometimes with heavy meals late in the day.
current_general_status.sleep: Variable due to study schedule; averages 5 to 6 hours with intermittent insomnia, which can precede seizure breakthrough.

persona: He is a 72-year-old man who has been living without stable housing for years and usually gets by with odd jobs, still thinking of himself as a hands-on mechanic and always looking for extra income. He speaks quickly and anxiously, spilling into long side stories about being scammed, checking BBC News, and worrying that every flare means something deadly, while repeatedly asking for reassurance. Despite the stress, he stays polite and appreciative, emphasizes that family matters most, and strongly prefers aggressive early action and clear virtual check-ins because keeping appointments is hard.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent worsening shortness of breath with increased cough and sputum for about 3 weeks after a recent respiratory infection-like illness, on a background of chronic symptoms for years.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: He reports a long-standing daily cough with intermittent wheeze and exertional dyspnea for several years, which has flared repeatedly over the past year. About 3 weeks ago he developed a gradually worsening cough with thicker sputum and more breathlessness than usual, along with chest tightness, fatigue, and low-grade subjective fevers and chills. Over the last week he notes reduced exercise tolerance, needing to stop to catch his breath with minimal activity, and occasional lightheadedness. Sputum has increased in volume and is more difficult to clear; he denies frank hemoptysis. He reports decreased appetite and early satiety, contributing to poor intake. He denies sharp pleuritic chest pain, unilateral leg swelling, sudden onset severe dyspnea, or syncope. He denies high spiking fevers or rigors, and denies known recent aspiration. No new orthopnea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea is reported. No focal neurologic deficits. He was recently in a minor traffic accident as a passenger and has residual chest wall soreness that makes coughing uncomfortable but does not fully explain the breathing symptoms. He also reports missing his blood pressure medications for several days due to poor time management and disrupted routines after being a scam victim, increasing stress and interfering with appointments.

current_general_status.diet: Poor appetite with low overall intake; reports previously being advised to follow a low-salt, "renal-style" diet as general hypertension-related dietary guidance (not due to known chronic kidney disease), but currently struggles to meet calorie and protein needs.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to coughing and shortness of breath; naps during the day.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination and bowel movements generally regular; occasional constipation when intake is poor.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss over recent months with low muscle mass; reports feeling weaker and appearing thin.

previous_diseases: Hypertension
Chronic bronchitis with recurrent winter respiratory flare-ups
Hyperlipidemia
medical_history.long_term_medication: Long-term meds include amlodipine 5 mg daily and losartan 50 mg daily for hypertension and atorvastatin 20 mg nightly for hyperlipidemia; uses intermittent inhaled bronchodilator as needed. He missed his blood pressure medications for several days recently.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Recent minor motor-vehicle accident with chest wall bruising; no known major surgeries reported.
infectious_diseases: Seasonal viral respiratory infections with prolonged cough in prior years
medical_history.vaccinations: Reports routine childhood vaccines; unsure of recent influenza and pneumococcal vaccination status.

persona: He is a 2-year-old boy in daycare, with a history of frequent respiratory infections and ongoing immune-supportive care, and his parents watch him closely for any signs he is struggling to breathe. Note: the recorded name in this synthetic/de-identified record may not match gender presentation. His caregiver speaks rapidly and at length, jumping between symptom details, daycare exposures, and worries about worst-case outcomes, repeatedly asking for reassurance and sometimes interrupting to clarify fears. Even while anxious, they try to be methodical, asking about likelihoods and what to monitor, and they rely on the patient portal for updates because juggling work demands and family time feels precarious right now.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Developed fever and persistent dry cough starting 4 days ago after exposure to sick children at daycare.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Began with low-grade fever and fatigue, then progressed over the next 2 days to recurrent higher fevers and a frequent dry cough that is worse at night and disrupts play. Has sore throat suggested by decreased appetite and increased irritability, intermittent nasal/throat discomfort, and occasional post-tussive gagging. Mild chest discomfort is suspected when coughing hard. Although Mycoplasma pneumonia is less common in toddlers than in school-aged children, it can occur in this age group, particularly with daycare exposure; the positive NAAT supports Mycoplasma pneumoniae as a likely causative pathogen. Consideration is also given to possible concurrent viral respiratory infection given age and exposure risk. No barking cough or stridor, no wheezing noted by caregivers, no choking episode or suspected foreign body ingestion, no vomiting or diarrhea, no neck stiffness, no seizures, and no cyanosis. Caregivers deny known sick contacts with tuberculosis.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced oral intake for 3 days; still taking small amounts of fluids.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep due to nighttime coughing and fever.
current_general_status.excretion: Urine output slightly decreased; stools normal.
current_general_status.weight_change: No clear weight change noted over the past week.

previous_diseases: Impaired immune function due to a primary immunodeficiency with recurrent respiratory infections in early childhood
medical_history.long_term_medication: Receives immune-supportive therapy and infection prophylaxis as directed by immunology (caregiver reports daily medication but does not recall names).
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: None.
medical_history.vaccinations: Reported up to date for age, including influenza this season; family unsure about timing of the most recent doses.

personal_history.obstetric_history: Born at term via uncomplicated vaginal delivery; normal newborn course; no NICU stay reported.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: One paternal cousin with a diagnosed primary immunodeficiency; otherwise none reported.

persona: He is a 64-year-old tattoo shop owner with limited financial breathing room who tends to “tough it out,” downplaying discomfort and insisting he’s fine until someone he trusts pushes him to get checked. Having lived through armed conflict earlier in life, he keeps a steady exterior, but lately he feels unsettled and is quietly searching for answers while trying not to burden friends or clients. In conversation he may sound indifferent at first, yet he’s curious and group-oriented, asking many clarifying questions and hoping for an empathetic, respectful bedside manner.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent protrusion from the anus during bowel movements with worsening perianal itching for about 18 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Symptoms began gradually with a feeling of anal heaviness and intermittent tissue protruding during straining that initially reduced on its own; over the past 6 months the protrusion has become more frequent and sometimes requires manual reduction, accompanied by a persistent sensation of incomplete evacuation. Over the same period, he developed intense perianal itching that is worse at night and after bowel movements, with frequent moisture and sticky mucus staining underwear; scratching has led to soreness and occasional superficial bleeding on wiping. He reports intermittent clear mucus seepage but denies black stools, large-volume rectal bleeding, fever, chills, acute severe abdominal pain, sudden inability to pass stool or gas, purulent drainage, or new focal neurologic deficits. No recent travel or new sexual exposures; no new soaps/detergents noted.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meals due to work; low fiber intake and limited water intake on busy days; uses coffee frequently.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to nocturnal itching and grief-related insomnia.
current_general_status.excretion: Chronic straining with prolonged time on the toilet; alternating hard stools and periods of loose stool; urinary hesitancy and weak stream with nocturia, often strains to void; chronic cough episodes increase abdominal pressure.
current_general_status.weight_change: Stable by home smart scale tracking, no significant unintentional change.

previous_diseases: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with chronic straining to urinate
Chronic bronchitis with recurrent cough

persona: He is a 78-year-old orchard owner who still stays part-time involved, leaning on hands-on experience and routine rather than online tools, and he often needs help using patient portals. He is married with two adult children and values keeping peace in the family, tending to make decisions by talking things through with others and sharing neighborhood updates. In appointments he is direct and factual, using plain words and answering questions clearly, but he can come across anxious and sensitive to feeling judged, especially when discussing diet choices, refills, or missed follow-ups.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressive exertional shortness of breath and palpitations with worsening leg swelling over the past 3 weeks, recurrent episodes over several months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past several months he reports intermittent episodes of chest tightness and reduced exercise tolerance, typically worsening after periods of higher-salt meals and hot weather days. Over the last 3 weeks symptoms have become more frequent and persistent, with palpitations (brief runs of rapid or irregular beats), exertional dyspnea now occurring with routine household activity, and needing extra pillows at night on some nights. He also notes increasing bilateral lower-extremity edema, limb fatigue, and episodes of generalized muscle weakness with occasional calf cramps. Accompanying symptoms include abdominal bloating, reduced appetite, and intermittent nausea. He denies fever, productive cough, pleuritic chest pain, syncope, focal neurologic deficits, black or bloody stools, and recent vomiting or severe diarrhea. No new unilateral leg pain or swelling is reported.

current_general_status.diet: High sodium intake by habit (salted and processed foods); appetite decreased recently; occasionally uses "holistic" supplements such as herbal teas but no consistent potassium-rich diet.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented by breathlessness at times; occasionally sleeps propped up; no recent nightmares.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination increased after diuretic doses; no dysuria; bowel movements regular though sometimes constipated with abdominal distension.
current_general_status.weight_change: Reports fluctuating weight with recent gain and tighter shoes over the past 2 weeks.

Hypertension

medical_history.long_term_medication: Long-term loop diuretic use (furosemide) with periodic dose increases during fluid retention; also takes antihypertensives and a statin. Prior evaluations suggest RAAS activation/possible increased aldosterone activity, which together with diuretic exposure can contribute to renal potassium wasting even with CKD; document timing of diuretic dosing relative to any urine electrolyte collection when interpreting urinary K and linking to hypokalemia etiology. Reports occasional difficulty obtaining timely refills due to insurance coverage limits and prior authorization delays.

medical_history.vaccinations: Receives annual influenza vaccine; completed pneumococcal vaccination series; COVID-19 boosters up to date per patient.

persona: He is a 90-year-old man who once worked as a genetic researcher and still carries himself like the head of a long-established family, quietly proud of the land he manages and the people he supports. He is inclusive in his outlook and tends to admire others easily, speaking in a straightforward, even-handed way about decisions, but he is a poor historian about his own body, describing things as “off” or “uncomfortable” with a muddled timeline. He prefers a question-and-answer back-and-forth, keeps up with regular checkups, and uses an Android phone mainly for practical tasks.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent right upper abdominal fullness and dull pain with worsening abdominal distension over the past 3 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last several months he has had intermittent dull, aching discomfort in the right upper quadrant with a sense of fullness, gradually becoming more frequent and lasting longer. He reports progressive abdominal bloating, early satiety, intermittent nausea, and looser stools after fatty meals. He notes these post-fatty-meal loose stools have been intermittent and not associated with fever, recent travel, or infectious symptoms; given abdominal ultrasound/CT showing no biliary ductal dilation and findings consistent with passive hepatic congestion, obstructive hepatobiliary causes are considered less likely at this time and the bowel habit change is treated as a possible separate/functional issue or secondary effect of congestion/ascites to be reassessed if it persists. Over the past 4 weeks he has noticed increasing ankle swelling and mild shortness of breath on exertion, worse when lying flat, with occasional nighttime awakening to catch his breath. He notes reduced exercise tolerance and increasing dependence on a wheelchair for longer distances due to generalized weakness and muscle wasting. Denies fever, chills, vomiting blood, black/tarry stools, severe sharp abdominal pain, yellowing of the eyes/skin, chest pain, cough with sputum, pleuritic pain, or recent travel-related diarrheal illness.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite with early satiety; prefers small meals; greasy foods worsen bloating and loose stools.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep with occasional orthopnea; uses extra pillows.
current_general_status.excretion: Intermittent loose stools; no blood noted; urination somewhat reduced on days with more swelling.
current_general_status.weight_change: Gradual unintentional weight loss over 6 months with noticeable muscle loss; abdominal girth increasing.

persona: She is a 53-year-old nightclub manager who has been proud of building a stable, successful life while keeping her personal information private and secure online. Widowed and still steadying herself emotionally, she stays close to her community and speaks warmly about her hometown, even as she leans on a small circle of friends she calls her “study group.” When she talks with clinicians, she is direct and factual, using plain words to describe what her hand, walking, sleep, and bathroom habits have been like, and she appreciates frequent status updates. She is also careful about cleanliness and encourages good handwashing.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Gradual onset of intermittent right-hand shaking at rest and increasing slowness with walking and daily tasks over the past 14 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: About 14 months ago, she began noticing a subtle, intermittent tremor in the right hand when relaxed (more noticeable when sitting or watching TV), which lessens when she reaches for objects. Over the past 8 months, she has developed increasing slowness in fine motor tasks (buttoning, typing), smaller handwriting, and generalized stiffness, especially in the shoulders and right arm. In the past 4 months, family and coworkers have commented on reduced facial expressiveness and a softer voice; she reports a slower, slightly shuffling gait and occasional imbalance when turning quickly, without a clear fall in the last year. She also reports constipation with bowel movements every 3 to 4 days, reduced sense of smell for several years (cannot detect coffee or perfume as well), intermittent lightheadedness on standing, and fragmented sleep with vivid dreams and occasional talking/movements at night. Mood has been persistently low with anxiety related to declining performance, and she reports misplacing personal items more often than before but remains independent in finances and work responsibilities. As part of an academic/research protocol and to help evaluate alternative etiologies of parkinsonism, CSF dopamine metabolite testing (including homovanillic acid) was obtained in addition to standard imaging. She denies fever, recent infection, severe headache, new unilateral weakness, numbness, facial droop, seizures, acute confusion, visual loss, back pain, or new bowel/bladder incontinence; no clear medication trigger for tremor and no heavy caffeine intake change.

current_general_status.diet: Balanced diet with preference for high-protein meals; trying to add fiber after constipation worsened; appetite intact.
current_general_status.sleep: Non-restorative sleep with frequent awakenings and vivid dreams; occasional nighttime movements reported by a family member.
current_general_status.excretion: Constipation with bowel movement every 3 to 4 days; mild urinary hesitancy at times; no dysuria or hematuria.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss of about 2 kg over 6 months.

Generalized anxiety disorder

persona: She is a 24-year-old music professional working in digital streaming, recognizable by her red hair and a sensitive, people-oriented personality shaped by community service commitments and limited financial flexibility. She tries to stay on top of preventive care when she can, but she is not very tech-savvy and can feel overwhelmed by portals, apps, or forms. In conversation she tends to describe things as feeling "off" or "weird" with a jumbled timeline, often forgetting details and having more trouble focusing when she feels unwell, so she relies on gentle prompting to pin down specifics.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: During a scheduled annual physical 2 weeks ago after an employer reminder, she reported sudden change over the prior 6 to 8 weeks with missed/irregular periods and new episodes of heat intolerance with sweating and fatigue.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last 6 to 8 weeks she developed oligomenorrhea progressing to a missed period, with intermittent hot flashes and night sweats (vasomotor episodes), vaginal dryness, reduced libido, and mood lability with anxiety/irritability consistent with hypoestrogenism/primary ovarian insufficiency. During these hot-flash episodes she sometimes notices brief palpitations, but she denies persistent tachycardia, tremor, or ongoing heat intolerance outside of the vasomotor spells. Around the same time she also noticed increasing fatigue, slowed thinking and forgetfulness at work, cold intolerance, dry skin, diffuse hair shedding, mild weight gain despite similar intake, and a sensation of neck fullness without acute pain, which are more consistent with hypothyroidism. She also reports decreased exercise tolerance and generalized muscle aches. Accompanying symptoms include constipation and low energy on waking. She denies fever, persistent sore throat, cough, chest pain, syncope, focal neurologic deficits, severe headaches or visual changes, galactorrhea, pelvic pain, abnormal vaginal discharge, dysuria, or heavy vaginal bleeding. She denies recent pregnancy, and denies use of hormonal contraception or emergency contraception in the last 3 months.

current_general_status.sleep: Non-restorative sleep with frequent nighttime awakenings and occasional night sweats over the past month.
current_general_status.excretion: Constipation with bowel movements every 2 to 3 days; no blood in stool; urination normal.

persona: She is a 1-year-old girl whose caregiver values honesty and prefers clear, transparent conversations, giving short, factual answers in everyday words. The family is seeking affordable housing and relies on app-based rideshares to get to daycare and clinic visits, trying to keep routines steady despite frequent colds and occasional itchy, dry skin. The caregiver is cooperative but carries some past stress and can become guarded at first, then relaxes when the plan is explained plainly. They keep connected through an online biology community and like making choices in a straightforward, bid-style way when options are offered.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent small, pearly bumps on the trunk and inner thighs first noticed about 3 months ago, with new bumps appearing again over the past 2 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Caregiver reports multiple discrete, smooth, shiny, skin-colored to pink dome-shaped papules, several with a tiny central dimple, ranging from pinhead to a few millimeters. Lesions initially appeared on the lower abdomen and groin folds and later spread to the inner thighs and axillae. Some bumps resolve on their own but new ones continue to appear, especially after daycare days. Mild intermittent scratching and surrounding dry, red, rough patches suggest irritant/eczema-like changes, but most bumps are not painful. No fever, no lethargy, no cough, no vomiting or diarrhea, no rapidly expanding warmth/redness suggesting cellulitis, and no grouped fluid-filled blisters or honey-colored crusting.

current_general_status.diet: Picky eater; drinks milk and eats small portions; appetite slightly decreased during flares but generally adequate.
current_general_status.sleep: Occasionally wakes scratching at night; otherwise age-appropriate sleep.

persona: She is a 74-year-old woman living on a stable income after years advocating for legal services and community fairness, and she still keeps busy with small volunteer commitments through her inclusive faith community. Separated and independent, she dislikes "making a fuss" and tends to minimize symptoms, often insisting she's fine until a family member pushes her to get checked. She is strictly vegan, avoids flying due to a longstanding fear, and relies on familiar routines; she struggles with new online patient portals, rarely reads health handouts, and admits she sometimes leans on energy drinks to get through tired days.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Gradually worsening blurred vision with intermittent eye pressure and colored halos for about 9 months, with recurrent flare-ups over the past 6 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: She reports progressive decline in vision in both eyes (worse in the right), increasing difficulty reading and recognizing faces, and more glare at night. Over the past several months she has noticed intermittent episodes of eye fullness and dull aching pain with frontal headache and colored halos around lights, often worse in the evening or after prolonged screen use; episodes have become more frequent in the last 6 weeks and occasionally are associated with mild nausea but no persistent vomiting. Photophobia is present during flares. She also describes patchy peripheral vision difficulty such as bumping into objects on the side and needing brighter light to see clearly. Given the episodic halos/headache/nausea pattern and prior exam findings of shallow anterior chambers with intermittent corneal epithelial edema and narrow angles, symptoms are discussed with the patient as consistent with intermittent/incipient angle-closure or lens-induced crowding that may coexist with chronic glaucomatous optic neuropathy, rather than a purely open-angle mechanism alone. She denies sudden complete vision loss, flashing lights with a curtain-like shadow, new floaters, ocular trauma, purulent discharge, fever, jaw claudication, scalp tenderness, focal weakness, or speech changes.

current_general_status.diet: Appetite fair; working on carbohydrate control and portion sizes while planning meals.
current_general_status.sleep: Light, fragmented sleep, partly due to eye discomfort and stress.

persona: He is a 48-year-old man who has been underemployed lately, picking up part-time work as an advisor or representative after years in a chain store job, and money has been tight enough that he sometimes stays in temporary shelter. He sounds worn out but stays cooperative and practical, preferring cautious, conservative treatment and leaning on advice from trusted experts before making decisions. He values charity and keeping close to family traditions, and although he is single, he is raising four or more kids while carrying quiet grief from losing a baby years ago. When asked, he answers in simple, direct terms about what he can and cannot do day to day.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent shortness of breath and intermittent chest tightness for about 6 months, worsened over the past 3 weeks after exertion.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 6 months he has had episodic fatigue with low-grade subjective fevers and intermittent itchy rash, followed by gradually increasing exertional dyspnea that has progressed to reduced exercise tolerance and occasional nighttime episodes of breathlessness requiring him to sit upright. Over the last 3 weeks, after brisk walking or climbing stairs he develops chest tightness or pressure with palpitations and lightheadedness, and he has noticed new bilateral ankle swelling by evening and a sense of abdominal bloating. He reports occasional pleuritic chest discomfort but no syncope. He also describes brief episodes of transient numbness in the right hand lasting minutes without persistent weakness. Based on the accompanying echocardiography/CMR findings (restrictive filling physiology with endocardial/subendocardial involvement and apical thrombus), his progressive dyspnea/edema and episodic chest symptoms are assessed as most consistent with heart failure presumed secondary to eosinophilic endomyocardial involvement (Loffler-type endomyocardial disease/endomyocardial fibrosis with thrombus) rather than isolated nonspecific heart failure alone. He denies productive cough, hemoptysis, wheezing-predominant attacks, recent upper respiratory infection, high fever with rigors, unilateral leg swelling, recent long-distance travel, sudden tearing back pain, black stools, or vomiting blood.

current_general_status.diet: Trying to follow a low-salt, fluid-conscious diet when possible; compliance is inconsistent due to limited food options in temporary shelter.
current_general_status.sleep: Sleep is fragmented; uses two pillows and occasionally needs to sleep sitting up due to breathlessness.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination somewhat decreased on days with more swelling; bowel movements regular.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional gain of about 3 kg over the past month with noticeable swelling.

previous_diseases: Myeloproliferative neoplasm with clonal eosinophilic proliferation (FIP1L1-PDGFRA mutation documented previously)
Recurrent episodes of immune dysregulation with flares of pruritic rash and elevated inflammatory markers
medical_history.long_term_medication: Intermittent courses of oral corticosteroids for inflammatory flares; adherence is variable. Not consistently taking any long-term targeted therapy; uses over-the-counter antihistamines as needed. No regular cardiac medications prior to current symptoms.

demographics.name: Kathleen Morales
demographics.occupation: Part-time environmental health worker (rodent and flea control)

persona: He is a 30-year-old man from a middle-class, multigenerational household who does part-time rodent and flea control work and cares quietly about protecting local ecosystems, especially in national parks. In conversation he is cooperative but hard to pin down, describing things as feeling "off" or "weird" and mixing up whether symptoms started "a few days ago" or "maybe last week" unless guided with specific questions. He tends to sound emotionally worn out and a bit detached from hands-on problem-solving, yet he seeks connection and responds well to a calm, respectful tone, showing a strong personal streak of integrity in how he wants his story understood.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Abrupt onset of high fever with severe headache starting 6 days ago after several days of rodent and flea control work in a high-rodent-density neighborhood.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Began suddenly with chills, malaise, and a persistent fever peaking around 39.0-39.5 C, partially relieved by acetaminophen but returning within hours. Over the next 2-3 days he developed worsening diffuse headache with dizziness, marked fatigue, poor concentration, and difficulty sleeping; he reports being easily distracted by environmental noise more than usual. On day 5 he noticed a new pink, blanching rash on the chest and upper abdomen that spread to the back and upper arms within a day; he has not noticed rash on the face, palms, or soles. Gastrointestinal symptoms include reduced appetite, intermittent nausea, two episodes of non-bloody, non-bilious vomiting, and mild crampy upper abdominal discomfort without severe localized pain. He denies cough, sore throat, runny nose, shortness of breath, chest pain, dysuria, focal weakness, neck stiffness, photophobia, persistent confusion, seizures, black stools, or bloody diarrhea. No known recent travel outside Jakarta in the last month and no known sick contacts; he reports frequent exposure to rodents and possible flea bites during work, especially in poorly sanitized households.

current_general_status.diet: Poor appetite; taking mostly porridge, soup, and oral rehydration fluids; nausea limits intake.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep with insomnia since fever onset; reports burnout and increased irritability.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination normal; bowel movements slightly decreased due to reduced intake; no diarrhea.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional loss of about 1-2 kg over the past week.

previous_diseases: Major depressive disorder, in remission
medical_history.long_term_medication: None routinely; uses acetaminophen as needed for fever and headache. No current antidepressant use.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: No prior surgeries. No major physical trauma reported; witnessed domestic violence in childhood.
medical_history.vaccinations: Childhood immunizations reported as up to date; received seasonal influenza vaccine last year; no recent travel vaccines.

personal_history.alcohol: Occasional alcohol on weekends, 1-2 drinks; none since illness began.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: Mother with type 2 diabetes; otherwise none known.

persona: A 72-year-old retired logistics expert, she lives with her spouse and prides herself on tradition and honoring family roots, even as her visual limitations and forgetfulness lead her to misplace keys or glasses in their dusty home. She is shy in the room and insists she is "fine," minimizing how much the injury rattled her, but she can become incensed when she feels her concerns are dismissed or time is wasted. Despite substantial resources, she prefers decisions grounded in clinical evidence links and asks for discharge instructions in plain language, with a practical focus on improving her blood pressure self-monitoring.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Severe left wrist pain and swelling with new hand numbness after a fall onto an outstretched hand about 6 hours ago; came to the emergency department for evaluation.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: After tripping at home and landing on the palm with the wrist extended, she developed immediate severe wrist pain with visible deformity and rapidly increasing swelling. Over the next few hours she noticed tightness around the wrist and a growing bruise with a firm, tender lump consistent with a hematoma. She also developed numbness and tingling with a burning sensation in the thumb, index finger, middle finger, and the radial side of the ring finger, along with reduced grip strength and difficulty pinching and holding objects. Symptoms are concerning for acute median nerve compression/carpal tunnel syndrome secondary to fracture swelling/hematoma. Pain worsens with wrist movement and hand elevation provides only mild relief. She denies open wound, fever, chills, chest pain, shortness of breath, head injury, loss of consciousness, neck pain, new facial droop, slurred speech, or numbness extending above the wrist. She denies cold, pale fingers, and reports capillary refill seems normal to her.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite since injury; typically follows a high-salt diet.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep due to wrist pain since the fall.
current_general_status.excretion: No acute changes in bowel or urinary habits.
current_general_status.weight_change: No recent unintentional weight change reported.

persona: She is a 22-year-old seasonal scout who helps manage a shared household and tries to live healthfully, though late nights and irregular meals often get in the way. Skeptical and wary of medical visits, she downplays symptoms with a quiet "it's nothing" attitude and only agreed to come in after a family member insisted. When stress builds, she retreats into sketching and small gardening projects, and she likes experimenting with essential oils as part of her routine. She prefers straightforward explanations and struggles with patient portals, so she appreciates printed instructions and simple follow-ups. She reports periodic rural stays/visits with routine exposure to dogs (including stray dogs) around homes/farms and occasional proximity to sheep/goats and other livestock in the area.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Intermittent right upper abdominal discomfort with bloating and reduced appetite recurring over the past 8 months, worsening over the last 3 weeks after returning from a rural home visit where she had frequent contact with dogs and farm animals.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: She reports a dull, pressure-like pain and fullness in the right upper quadrant and epigastrium, initially occurring a few times per month and now present most days. Symptoms are worse after meals and when lying on the right side, and she notes early satiety, nausea, and occasional non-bilious vomiting. Over the last 3 weeks she has developed intermittent itching and dark urine with brief episodes of yellowing of the eyes, and feels more fatigued than usual. Baseline labs obtained at initial evaluation show a cholestatic pattern consistent with these episodes: total bilirubin 2.6 mg/dL (direct 1.9 mg/dL), ALP 312 U/L, GGT 286 U/L, AST 74 U/L, ALT 98 U/L. She denies high fever or rigors, denies persistent diarrhea, denies chest pain, irritative cough, hemoptysis, or shortness of breath, and has had no episodes of sudden severe abdominal pain with fainting or generalized hives. No recent travel outside her region; no known sick contacts.

current_general_status.diet: Decreased appetite with early satiety; tends to snack irregularly and often skips meals when busy.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep with frequent late nights and sleep deprivation.
current_general_status.excretion: Intermittent constipation; urine sometimes darker during symptomatic episodes; no persistent pale stools reported. Baseline LFTs at initial evaluation show cholestatic hyperbilirubinemia (total/direct bilirubin 2.6/1.9 mg/dL) with elevated ALP and GGT.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss of about 2 kg over 2 months.

persona: She is a 63-year-old homeowner who takes pride in staying independent and keeping her life organized, and she still works in a supervisory advisor role where she naturally offers practical suggestions to others. In conversation she is direct and factual, using plain words and answering questions clearly, but she will point out quickly when something hurts because she has a low tolerance for pain. She sees herself as a survivor after past health scares and leans on steady routines, occasionally checking reddit health forums for shared experiences and tips; she usually gets to appointments by rideshare rather than driving.

present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Three days ago she developed a new persistent dry cough that has become more frequent, with several episodes of blood-streaked sputum since yesterday. She reports dull right-sided chest tightness that worsens with coughing and mild shortness of breath on exertion; she also notes new hoarseness and increased fatigue. Appetite has been reduced over the past week. No high fever or chills, no pleuritic sharp pain, no wheezing attacks typical of asthma, no leg swelling or calf pain, no syncope, and no recent upper-respiratory infection symptoms such as sore throat or nasal congestion.

persona: She is a 50-year-old family court judge who rents and prides herself on fairness and journalistic integrity, often reading long-form investigations in depth and thinking in careful, long-range terms. She arrives convinced the internet has already solved her case, confidently naming a diagnosis and pushing for specific tests or medications while brushing aside details that do not match her theory, and she can become defensive if challenged. Despite an analytical style, she is sentimental about family and wants them included in care discussions, peppering clinicians with clarifying questions and trying to regain control by learning practical routines like low-sodium cooking as her stamina has declined.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressive shortness of breath with exertion and intermittent dizziness for about 10 months, noticeably worse over the past 6 weeks after climbing stairs at work.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Initially noticed getting winded on hills and during fast walking, now becomes short of breath with one flight of stairs and sometimes at rest when rushing between jobs. Reports marked fatigue, intermittent chest tightness with exertion that improves with rest, occasional palpitations, and several episodes of lightheadedness with brief transient visual dimming (about 1-2 times per week) but no complete loss of consciousness. Over the last 2 months, developed a dry, irritating cough and ankle swelling that is mild but noticeable by evening (trace to 1+ at ankles, improves overnight); no abdominal distension reported. Notes reduced exercise tolerance and needing to pause to catch her breath. Denies fever, chills, purulent sputum, wheezing, pleuritic chest pain, calf pain or sudden unilateral leg swelling, hemoptysis, or recent long-distance travel. No orthopnea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea reported, and no positional worsening of dyspnea, despite objective evidence of significant right-heart strain. No known recent viral illness. Hoarseness occurs intermittently after prolonged talking at work.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meals due to multiple jobs; reduced appetite on more symptomatic days; tends to choose convenience foods.
current_general_status.sleep: Light and fragmented sleep with early awakening; sleeps about 5 to 6 hours nightly; sometimes wakes feeling unrefreshed.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements regular; urinates infrequently during work shifts and often notices darker urine, consistent with low fluid intake.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss of about 3 kg over the past 6 months.

Genetic testing positive for BMPR2 mutation and BMP9 mutation (identified during family evaluation); variants are treated as risk factors/associations with incomplete penetrance/uncertain pathogenicity in this dataset, and the case remains labeled idiopathic PAH per record classification.

demographics.name: Matthew Stewart
demographics.occupation: Data processor in the semiconductor industry

persona: He is a 31-year-old data processor in the semiconductor industry who has been financially strained lately and relies on public transport to get to work and appointments. Anxious and run down from poor sleep, he copes by researching symptoms online and comes in convinced he already knows what is happening, pushing for specific tests or medications while brushing off details that do not match his theory. He prefers plain-language explanations and frequently steers the conversation toward minimizing out-of-pocket costs. Despite low motivation day to day, he values rehabilitation and leans on connections through the Oxford Black Alumni Network for practical advice.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent burning retrosternal and upper abdominal pain with heartburn and painful swallowing, worsening over the past 3 weeks after a period of irregular meals and frequent very hot/spicy foods.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past year, has had intermittent episodes of dull, burning retrosternal pain and epigastric discomfort often triggered by eating (especially very hot soups/tea and spicy or acidic foods) and worsened when lying down at night or bending forward. In the last 3 weeks symptoms have become more frequent and intense, with prominent heartburn, sour/acid regurgitation, frequent belching, nausea, and a sensation of food sticking behind the sternum, especially with solid foods. Reports intermittent odynophagia (pain with swallowing) and avoidance of meals due to discomfort, contributing to fatigue and poor sleep from nocturnal symptoms. Denies fever, chills, persistent cough, wheezing, or shortness of breath. Denies exertional chest pressure, palpitations, syncope, or radiation of pain to the left arm. No persistent vomiting, no black tarry stools, and no vomiting of blood; notes occasional throat irritation/hoarseness in the mornings. No recent foreign body ingestion or caustic chemical exposure reported. Given odynophagia and the discrete distal esophageal ulcer, infectious esophagitis (HSV/CMV/Candida) and pill-related injury are considered in the differential; however, the endoscopic appearance and biopsy report of nonspecific necrosis/inflammation without described viral cytopathic effect or fungal elements make these less likely at present, with reevaluation/targeted organism assessment if symptoms fail to improve.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meal timing with occasional late-night overeating; frequently consumes very hot beverages/foods and spicy/irritating meals; reduced intake recently due to pain with swallowing.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep with nighttime burning/regurgitation; often wakes and needs to sit upright.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements regular; no reported melena; urination normal.
current_general_status.weight_change: Mild unintentional weight loss over the past month due to reduced appetite and smaller meals.
