persona: She is a 52-year-old artificial intelligence expert living in a high-income area, usually self-reliant and reluctant to make a fuss, often insisting she is "fine" even when clearly run down. After a past workplace industrial accident and with a recent fall now limiting her mobility and transportation, she feels quietly overwhelmed but remains hopeful, leaning on her spouse to push her into seeking care. She is skeptical of commercialization and prefers straightforward explanations over salesy pitches, asking pointed questions about side effects and interactions while still valuing justice, forgiveness, and practical solutions. Health-conscious and ingredient-focused, she used to stay active with canoe slalom and keeps a strong interest in renewable energy.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: New onset worsening cough with sputum and low-grade fever for 10 days, with blood-streaked sputum noted since 2 days ago after a recent close exposure at work.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Developed a dry cough that became productive over the past week, with yellow-white sputum and increasing fatigue. Reports intermittent afternoon low-grade fever, drenching night sweats, reduced appetite, and mild pleuritic right-sided chest pain with deep inspiration and coughing. Noted two episodes of blood-streaked sputum in the last 48 hours. Feels more short of breath than usual on exertion but can speak full sentences at rest. Denies sore throat, rhinorrhea, known aspiration event, leg swelling, calf pain, severe sudden tearing chest/back pain, or recent choking. No high-grade persistent fever, no purulent sinus symptoms, and no wheezing predominance. No confusion or neck stiffness.

current_general_status.diet: Decreased appetite over the last week; tries to choose "clean" foods and is cautious about additives; currently eating smaller portions.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep due to nocturnal sweats and coughing spells.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements regular; no diarrhea; urination normal without dysuria or hematuria.

persona: He is a lively kindergarten student from a financially stable immigrant family that values charity and often donates or helps neighbors, and he is usually doing well day to day. Since the swelling started, his caregiver becomes extremely anxious and talks rapidly, jumping between details about the kitten, school-night noise ruining his sleep, and alarming things they have read, repeatedly interrupting to ask if it could be cancer or something deadly. They can be opinionated and a bit irritable when worried, yet they prefer a conservative, step-by-step plan and are very tech-savvy, expecting easy online scheduling and quick updates.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Painful swelling under the left armpit with low-grade fever for about 5 days after a kitten scratch about 2 weeks ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: About 2 weeks ago, he was scratched on the left hand while playing with a kitten in the home; the family reports the kitten is under 1 year old and several household members handle it daily. Within a few days, a small red bump developed at the scratch site and slowly became a crusted papule. Over the past 5 days, he developed increasing tenderness and a gradually enlarging lump in the left axilla, with mild redness and warmth, discomfort with lifting the arm, and intermittent low-grade fever, decreased appetite, and fatigue. No sore throat, no cough, no runny nose, no ear pain, no dental pain, no generalized rash, no weight loss, no night sweats, and no recent travel. No eye redness or discharge, no headache, no confusion, no seizures, and no abdominal pain.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite compared with baseline; drinking fluids adequately.

current_general_status.excretion: Urination normal; stools slightly decreased in frequency but not painful; no diarrhea.

persona: He is a 61-year-old married man who does part-time communication consulting, having previously owned a beauty salon and once keeping up a disciplined training routine as a competitive athlete. He tries to live healthy on a tight budget, often weighing costs against appointments and routines, and he leans on technology to track things, search symptoms, and organize questions in a structured way. In conversation he can be raw and emotional, but also a poor historian, describing problems as feeling "off" or "weird" with a jumbled timeline and broad gestures rather than clear details, while relying on frequent coffee to get through long days.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Sudden onset of severe right eye pain with deep redness and light sensitivity starting 3 days ago, along with worsening joint pain and morning stiffness over the past 2 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Three days ago the patient developed abrupt, severe deep eye pain described as boring and radiating to the temple and jaw, worse at night and with eye movement, associated with marked redness with a violaceous hue, tearing, and photophobia; he reports mild blur but can still read large print. Over the last 2 weeks he has also had increased symmetric pain and swelling in the small joints of the hands and wrists with prolonged morning stiffness and reduced grip strength, making teaching and daily tasks difficult. He notes generalized fatigue and decreased activity tolerance. He denies purulent ocular discharge, recent contact lens use, foreign body sensation, facial vesicular rash, acute severe headache with nausea, focal neurologic deficits, recent eye trauma, fever or chills, and denies sudden complete vision loss.

current_general_status.diet: Low-carb focused; reduced appetite since eye pain started; tries to choose lower-cost options and sometimes skips meals when busy.
current_general_status.sleep: Poor sleep due to nocturnal eye pain and joint discomfort; wakes multiple times nightly.

Dry eyes and dry mouth symptoms intermittently

persona: She is a 15-year-old student from a lower-income household who tries to stay on top of her health and avoid problems before they get worse, mostly because she worries about paying for care and medications. In conversation she stays fairly neutral, but she can be a staunch defender of herself and asks critical questions once she feels heard. She is a poor historian and often describes symptoms as "weird" or "off," with a messy timeline and broad descriptions, partly due to a language barrier, and she hopes interpretation is available. Even while juggling school, pregnancy appointments, and diabetes routines, she still values charity and looks for small ways to give back.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent vulvar itching and thick white vaginal discharge worsening over the past 3 weeks, with similar episodes over the last 6 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the last 6 months, the patient reports intermittent episodes of intense vulvar pruritus that often becomes worse at night and has recurred multiple times; during the current flare for 3 weeks, itching has become more persistent with vulvar burning and soreness. She notes thick, white, clumpy vaginal discharge that sometimes increases after a warm bath. She also reports stinging discomfort with urination when urine contacts the irritated vulvar skin, and occasional pain with attempted intercourse (consensual; see sexual history). Scratching has led to mild excoriations and redness. Symptoms have not fully resolved between episodes despite intermittent use of over-the-counter topical products. She denies fever, lower abdominal or pelvic pain, flank pain, nausea/vomiting, generalized rash, genital vesicles, abnormal vaginal bleeding, or foul fishy odor.

current_general_status.diet: Variable appetite; often eats irregularly due to school and appointments, with frequent high-carbohydrate snacks despite attempts to follow a diabetes meal plan.
current_general_status.sleep: Sleeps less than 6 hours most nights; itching and anxiety about symptoms often disrupt sleep.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements generally regular; occasional loose stools and perianal irritation with concern for stool contamination of the vulvar area.
current_general_status.weight_change: No significant weight change noted in the past 2 months.

persona: She is a 28-year-old single woman who works part-time as a project management consultant while also helping support her family financially. Around people she knows, she can be charismatic and persuasive, but in clinics she gets visibly uneasy, especially about needles, and may hesitate before agreeing to tests. She values honesty and clear facts, and she talks in a direct, simple way: what she feels, where it shows up, and what seems to trigger it. Outside of work, she’s tied into a local fishing community and cares about reducing mental health stigma.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Gradually worsening net-like reddish-brown discoloration on the medial thighs and lower abdomen for about 8 months, occurring in areas where she regularly applies a heating pad for cramps, recurrent despite topical moisturizers.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Started as faint, intermittent redness after repeated localized heat exposure from a heating pad used directly against the skin, then slowly became persistent with a reticular (lacy) pattern and increasing brownish discoloration. Uses the heating pad in direct contact (or through a thin layer of clothing) over the lower abdomen and often resting across the medial thighs, typically ~45–60 minutes per session, about 4–5 days per week. Primarily involves the medial thighs and lower abdomen, with occasional mild burning/itching and skin dryness; reports one brief episode of small, tense blisters that resolved without drainage. The discoloration tends to fade slightly during periods when she uses the heating pad less (often in warmer months) and recurs/worsens when she restarts frequent heating pad use (more often in colder months). No severe pain, no spreading rash to face/hands, no fever, no purulent drainage, no recent new soaps/detergents, no oral ulcers, and no joint swelling.

persona: She is a 72-year-old widow and retired organizational effectiveness consultant who now paces herself with chores, resting often when fatigue and swelling slow her down. She speaks in a direct, factual way, giving clear timelines and simple descriptions, but she is cautious and sometimes defensive because she worries about surprises and feels regretful about earlier stretches of poor diabetes control. Living on a tight budget, she reads medication labels carefully, asks for transparent billing and cost estimates, and is diligent about sun protection. She likes group activities that support cultural preservation and community progress, and she has recently taken up baking as a gentle, rewarding hobby.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Progressively worsening fatigue, poor appetite, and leg/eyelid swelling over the past 3 months, with reduced urine output over the last 2 weeks.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past several months she has developed increasing asthenia and exercise intolerance, needing frequent rest for routine household tasks. Appetite has declined with intermittent nausea and a metallic taste; she reports occasional non-bloody vomiting in the last week. Over the last month she noticed pruritus that is worse at night and new swelling around the ankles and eyelids that improves only slightly with leg elevation. In the past 2 weeks she reports noticeably decreased urine volume and darker urine, along with mild shortness of breath when lying flat and on climbing one flight of stairs. Home blood pressure readings have been higher than usual. She denies fever, chills, productive cough, pleuritic chest pain, hemoptysis, calf pain, focal weakness, severe headache, dysuria, flank pain, or gross hematuria; no black stools or overt gastrointestinal bleeding. Urinalysis showing heavy proteinuria with microscopic hematuria is most consistent with advanced diabetic kidney disease in this context; a superimposed glomerular process is considered but is less likely and is not aggressively pursued given bilateral small echogenic kidneys with cortical thinning consistent with longstanding irreversible chronic parenchymal disease.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite; continues diabetes-conscious carbohydrate counting but has been eating smaller portions and limiting salty foods due to swelling.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to itching and nocturnal urination earlier in the course; now wakes less to urinate but feels unrefreshed.
current_general_status.excretion: Urine output decreased over the last 2 weeks; bowel movements every 1 to 2 days with occasional constipation; no diarrhea.
current_general_status.weight_change: Reports a gradual unintentional weight loss of about 3 kg over 3 months but notes fluctuating weight day-to-day with fluid retention.

persona: She is a 28-year-old exchange student who serves as a sports team captain, but lately she has had to scale back practices and archery because cold days and too much sun leave her wiped out and sore. She speaks in a direct, factual way and usually keeps to simple details like when her fingers change color, when the leg spots show up, and how often she feels tingling at night, though she can get a bit avoidant when talking about past pregnancy loss. Underfunded and careful about digital privacy, she prefers watching patient education videos and chooses options only after thinking them through, leaning on her group for support without making a big fuss.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent episodes of painful color changes in fingers with cold exposure and recurring purplish rash on the lower legs for about 9 months, worsening over the past 6 weeks after frequent outdoor sun exposure.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Over the past 9 months, she has had intermittent cold-triggered finger discoloration (white to blue to red) with numbness and burning pain, along with recurrent non-blanching purplish spots on the lower legs that last several days and sometimes leave residual darkening. In the last 6 weeks, symptoms have become more frequent with new small, tender sores at the fingertips and toes, increasing fatigue, and more prominent joint pain and morning stiffness in the hands and wrists. She also reports episodic facial redness across the cheeks after sun exposure, occasional low-grade fevers, and tingling in both feet that is worse at night. She has noticed foamy urine intermittently and mild ankle swelling late in the day. She denies chest pain, acute shortness of breath at rest, coughing up blood, severe abdominal pain, black or bloody stools, sudden one-sided weakness, severe headache, or new seizures. No recent travel, no known tick bite, and no new sexual partners reported.

current_general_status.sleep: Light and irregular sleep with difficulty maintaining a schedule; often uses a futon; symptoms and tingling can disrupt sleep.
current_general_status.excretion: Urination normal in frequency; occasional foamy urine; no dysuria. Bowel movements regular; no persistent diarrhea or constipation.

personal_history.obstetric_history: G1P1 with a prior stillbirth vs neonatal loss (gestational age not documented; outcome classification to be clarified). Not currently pregnant. Reports symptom fluctuations around menstrual cycles and during periods of hormonal contraception changes in the past.
personal_history.menstrual_history: Menarche at 12. Cycles generally regular with moderate flow; reports premenstrual symptom flares and increased fatigue around menses.

family_history.similar_illness: Mother with an autoimmune connective tissue disease with photosensitivity and arthritis symptoms; maternal aunt with recurrent cold-induced finger color changes.
family_history.hereditary_diseases: Known HLA-DR3 positivity in patient reported from prior rheumatology workup; otherwise no known inherited disorders in two generations.

persona: She is a 71-year-old woman on a fixed income who lives in a multigenerational household and prefers to present herself as "fine," downplaying symptoms until her family insists she get checked. Quietly analytical and strongly visual in how she thinks, she reads discharge instructions carefully and asks precise questions once she warms up, even if she initially seems stoic. Although she worries about being judged and carries some anxiety under the surface, she values diversity and inclusion and keeps herself steady with gentle routines, including modified aikido practice and careful skin-friendly habits.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Intermittent outward drifting of the right eye with occasional double vision and eye strain for about 2 years, gradually becoming more noticeable over the past 6 months.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Reports recurrent episodes where one eye appears to deviate, more apparent when tired, reading, or in bright sunlight. Describes intermittent horizontal diplopia at distance, associated with dizziness and nausea at times; she often closes one eye to relieve symptoms. Increasing visual fatigue with prolonged near work, and family has noticed frequent mild head turn to see clearly. Also reports blurred vision despite older glasses and difficulty with depth perception when walking on uneven surfaces. No eye redness, purulent discharge, severe eye pain, sudden curtain-like vision loss, new flashes/floaters, facial droop, new limb weakness, acute severe headache, fever, or recent sinus symptoms.

current_general_status.diet: Soft diet at times due to swallowing difficulty; otherwise regular meals with adequate appetite.
current_general_status.sleep: Light sleep with intermittent awakenings due to joint stiffness; no known sleep apnea.
current_general_status.excretion: No change in bowel habits; urinary frequency at night once to twice, unchanged.
current_general_status.weight_change: No significant unintentional weight change over the past 6 months.

previous_diseases: Cerebrovascular disease with ischemic stroke about 4 years ago with near-complete recovery
Remote mild traumatic brain injury from a fall about 3 years ago without neurosurgery
Ocular trauma to the right eye from a gardening accident about 1 year ago (treated in urgent care, no known globe rupture)
History of uncorrected hyperopia earlier in life with inconsistent use of glasses
History of uncorrected myopia in the other eye reported since midlife
Longstanding uncorrected astigmatism (glasses prescription not updated for several years)
Childhood measles infection (self-reported)
medical_history.long_term_medication: Aspirin daily and a statin for secondary stroke prevention; uses acetaminophen as needed for arthritis-related stiffness.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: No major surgeries; prior fall-related head injury as above; no known orbital surgery.
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date on influenza most years; received pneumococcal vaccine; uncertain about shingles vaccination.

personal_history.smoking: Former smoker; quit about 20 years ago after smoking for several decades.
personal_history.alcohol: Rare alcohol use (occasional wine).
personal_history.obstetric_history: G3P3; all term vaginal deliveries, no major peripartum complications reported. Currently lives in a multigenerational household.
personal_history.menstrual_history: Postmenopausal since her early 50s; no postmenopausal bleeding.

family_history.similar_illness: One grandchild has Down syndrome; no known family history of eye muscle disorders reported.
family_history.hereditary_diseases: Down syndrome in a grandchild; otherwise none known.

persona: He is a 7-year-old elementary student who usually tries to be brave and insists he is fine, answering questions in short phrases and downplaying discomfort unless his caregiver presses. He stays optimistic and wants to be honest, but he gets distracted and fidgety when the pain or itching flares, often needing the same instructions repeated and reassurance that he is not in trouble. He has sensitive skin and hates the feeling of dampness or frequent wiping, which makes him irritable at night. His parent manages a busy household and relies on rideshare for appointments.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: New onset of pain and swelling near the anus with yellowish drainage noted in underwear for 3 days, starting after several days of frequent diarrhea; diarrhea to be assessed for infectious cause versus Crohn flare.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Child developed increased stool frequency and loose stools about 1 week ago, followed by sudden perianal soreness and a tender lump 3 days ago. Within 24 hours, the area began to drain a small amount of yellow-white fluid with occasional blood streaking, temporarily easing the pain, but the discomfort returns when the opening seems to close. Caregiver reports itching and irritation around the anus and foul-smelling moisture requiring frequent wiping and clothing changes. Mild fatigue and decreased appetite since onset. No persistent high fever, no vomiting, no abdominal guarding, no urinary symptoms, no cough or respiratory symptoms, and no known recent perianal trauma. Given preceding diarrhea and Crohn history, evaluate for infectious gastroenteritis versus Crohn flare (review exposures and obtain stool studies if indicated) before using antimotility agents.

current_general_status.diet: Reduced appetite; tolerating small meals and protein-rich snacks; drinking fluids adequately.
current_general_status.sleep: Restless sleep due to perianal discomfort, waking at night to complain of pain/itching.
current_general_status.excretion: Ongoing diarrhea with urgency; no reported constipation; no visible large-volume rectal bleeding.
current_general_status.weight_change: Caregiver notes slight recent weight loss over the past few weeks.

previous_diseases: Crohn disease (diagnosed previously; intermittent flares with abdominal pain and diarrhea)
Perianal abscess (treated previously with drainage per caregiver report)
medical_history.long_term_medication: Maintenance therapy for Crohn disease (caregiver reports daily medication) and intermittent use of antidiarrheals as previously advised during flares; avoid antimotility agents unless directed by the treating clinician after infection/toxic colitis risk is ruled out.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Prior bedside/incision drainage of a perianal abscess; no other surgeries. No major trauma reported.
medical_history.vaccinations: Reported up to date for age.

personal_history.smoking: None (child). No household smoking reported by caregiver.
personal_history.alcohol: None (child).
personal_history.obstetric_history: Full-term birth per caregiver; no neonatal complications reported.

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None known.

persona: He is a 79-year-old retired zoologist who once did well financially and still carries himself with high standards, even when he feels worn down and a bit unsure of himself. With housing stress and his health problems piling up, he’s tense and wants clear, dependable answers, and he can come off as picky about what is “reliable” or “well-supported,” especially when it comes to technology and software working with his devices. He speaks in a direct, factual way and is comfortable addressing sensitive topics without dancing around them, but he prefers plain language and practical steps he can follow.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Gradually worsening cough with increasing thick sputum and intermittent low-grade fevers over the past 6 weeks, with recurrence after partial improvement 2 months ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Symptoms began as an intermittent dry cough and mild fatigue, then progressed over several weeks to daily cough with mucopurulent sputum and occasional blood-streaking, pleuritic right-sided chest discomfort, reduced exercise tolerance, and shortness of breath with exertion. He reports intermittent fevers with chills, poor appetite, and unintentional weight loss. Over the last 7–10 days, dyspnea has become more noticeable and he feels weaker. He notes recent exposure to heavy wildfire smoke while spending time outdoors and in poorly ventilated spaces. He denies acute sudden-onset chest pain, leg swelling, recent long-distance travel, orthopnea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, wheezing, known aspiration events, severe night sweats, or recent contact with persons known to have active tuberculosis.

current_general_status.diet: Plant-based friendly when possible; appetite reduced recently with smaller, irregular meals due to housing instability.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to nocturnal cough and stress; naps during the day.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements less regular than usual; urine output unchanged; no dysuria.
current_general_status.weight_change: Unintentional weight loss of about 4–6 kg over 2 months.

previous_diseases: HIV infection with prior opportunistic infections (details unclear to patient)
History of leukemia/lymphoma treated in the past
Status post solid-organ transplantation
Chronic kidney disease stage 3
Hypertension
Chronic bronchitis/COPD (mild, prior smoking-related)
medical_history.long_term_medication: On long-term immunosuppressants for transplant (e.g., tacrolimus and mycophenolate with low-dose prednisone per outside records), antiretroviral therapy for HIV, and antihypertensives; patient frequently forgets doses without cues and has inconsistent access to refills.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Organ transplant surgery years ago; right below-knee amputation with prosthetic use after remote trauma.
infectious_diseases: Recurrent pneumonias in the last few years
medical_history.blood_transfusion: Yes, received transfusions during prior cancer treatment (year not recalled).
medical_history.vaccinations: Incompletely up to date; reports influenza vaccination inconsistently and is unsure about pneumococcal and COVID boosters.

personal_history.smoking: Former smoker, about 25 pack-years; quit around age 60.
personal_history.alcohol: Rare alcohol use currently; denies heavy use.
personal_history.obstetric_history: None

family_history.hereditary_diseases: None known; family history limited due to estrangement.

demographics.name: Nicole Riley
demographics.occupation: Technical professional in doll manufacturing; novice in local politics

persona: He is a 40-year-old technical professional in doll manufacturing who has started dipping his toes into local politics, but lately his world has narrowed around pain, fatigue, and being careful not to fall. He lives in a shared household setup and is a new parent still adjusting to childcare, which makes his sleep worse and keeps him tense, especially after pandemic-related cancellations disrupted plans and income. He values keeping the peace and tends to bottle things up, so he is slow to ask for help even when he needs it. In visits he is direct and factual, describing what happened in simple terms and focusing on what he can see and feel day to day.

present_illness_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent low-impact fractures and worsening deep bone pain over the past 6 months, most recently after a minor fall 2 weeks ago.
present_illness_history.symptoms_and_progression: Reports years of intermittent aching pain in the long bones and lower back, with a noticeable increase in frequency and severity over the last 6 months. Over this period he has had multiple fractures after minimal trauma (e.g., twisting injury while stepping off a curb, bumping into a doorway), with prolonged pain and slow functional recovery after each event. He also notes increasing fatigue and reduced exercise tolerance, and has been told in the past he had low blood counts. Over the last year he has developed progressive visual difficulty (blurred vision and reduced peripheral awareness), and occasionally gets headaches and facial tingling. Denies fever, chronic cough, night sweats, unintended major weight loss, hemoptysis, focal chest pain, acute shortness of breath, new urinary symptoms, or acute single-joint redness/swelling suggestive of gout or septic arthritis.

current_general_status.diet: Irregular meals; tries to maintain balanced intake but appetite is reduced during pain flares.
current_general_status.sleep: Fragmented sleep due to bone pain; sleeps about 4 to 6 hours per night.
current_general_status.excretion: Bowel movements every 1 to 2 days; occasional constipation when taking pain medication; urination normal.
current_general_status.weight_change: Mild unintentional weight loss of about 3 to 5 pounds over 6 months.

previous_diseases: Recurrent fractures since adolescence (treated non-operatively and with occasional casting)
Chronic anemia noted intermittently on routine labs
Vision impairment (progressive over the last year)
medical_history.long_term_medication: Uses intermittent NSAIDs for pain; takes an over-the-counter vitamin D supplement. No regular prescription medications reported.
medical_history.surgery_or_trauma: Multiple prior fractures (wrist, ribs, and tibia) from minor trauma; no major surgeries reported.
medical_history.blood_transfusion: One prior packed red blood cell transfusion about 8 years ago during evaluation for symptomatic anemia.
medical_history.vaccinations: Up to date on routine adult vaccines per patient; influenza vaccine received this season.

personal_history.smoking: Former smoker; quit 10 years ago. No current tobacco use.
personal_history.alcohol: In sustained recovery from alcohol and polysubstance use for 4 years; currently abstinent and engaged in a recovery program.
personal_history.obstetric_history: Not applicable (male)

family_history.similar_illness: Positive: a younger sibling diagnosed with osteopetrosis; patient reports parental consanguinity (parents are first cousins).
family_history.hereditary_diseases: None
